data.title: Coleman Beach Shade
data.description: " Take your own shady, private space to the beach or park with the Coleman Beach Shade. This pop-up beach tent is made with UVGuard material that offers 50+ UPF protection from the suns harmful rays and durable Polyguard material that is designed to last season after season. The extended floor can be zipped shut for privacy while changing clothes, and the dry line offers a convenient place to hang wet bathing suits and towels. Integrated storage pockets give you a secure place to put your items while you swim or sunbathe. And when it gets hot, the back window can be unzipped to let the breeze in, while the sand bag compartments can be filled as needed to anchor the beach canopy on windy days. The Coleman Beach Shade comes with ground stakes and pre-attached guy lines and can be set up in about five minutes.
data.reviewText: This is a great tent for the beach. I can set it up by myself in <10min. I used it in the outer banks and it was fairly windy but if you dig down and really bury the sand anchors it has no issue in the wind. It packs down to nothing (the bag is a little bigger then a cling wrap box) which is great when you hauling everything to the beach.
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman Beach Shade

DESCRIPTION:
" Take your own shady, private space to the beach or park with the Coleman Beach Shade. This pop-up beach tent is made with UVGuard material that offers 50+ UPF protection from the suns harmful rays and durable Polyguard material that is designed to last season after season. The extended floor can be zipped shut for privacy while changing clothes, and the dry line offers a convenient place to hang wet bathing suits and towels. Integrated storage pockets give you a secure place to put your items while you swim or sunbathe. And when it gets hot, the back window can be unzipped to let the breeze in, while the sand bag compartments can be filled as needed to anchor the beach canopy on windy days. The Coleman Beach Shade comes with ground stakes and pre-attached guy lines and can be set up in about five minutes.

REVIEW:
This is a great tent for the beach. I can set it up by myself in <10min. I used it in the outer banks and it was fairly windy but if you dig down and really bury the sand anchors it has no issue in the wind. It packs down to nothing (the bag is a little bigger then a cling wrap box) which is great when you hauling everything to the beach.

data.title: Coleman Palmetto Cool Weather Adult Sleeping Bag
data.description: The Coleman Palmetto Sleeping Bag is a rectangular, cool weather sleeping bag made for camping temperatures between 30 and 50 degrees. Perfect for Family camping!.ThermoLock construction reduces heat loss through the zipper, keeping you warmer.ZipperGlide tailoring allows smooth zipper operation around the corners.QuickCord for easy, no tie closure.ComfortCuff surrounds your face with softness.Commercial Machine washable.
data.reviewText: Not a good sleeping bag if you ask me. I purchased this at Walmart for about the same price as it is here and I've only used it once. After only one night a large (about 9 inches) section of the seams where it is stitched came apart. The temperature rating I would say is about right on. With the unfortunate seam opening I was able to see the filler material inside and I highly doubt it is cotton as it is described. Going to return it and look for a 100% cotton inside and out. For the life of me I don't know why anyone would want that sticky poly material against their skin in any way inside or out. It feels really gross. Spend a bit more and get a better bag.
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman Palmetto Cool Weather Adult Sleeping Bag

DESCRIPTION:
The Coleman Palmetto Sleeping Bag is a rectangular, cool weather sleeping bag made for camping temperatures between 30 and 50 degrees. Perfect for Family camping!.ThermoLock construction reduces heat loss through the zipper, keeping you warmer.ZipperGlide tailoring allows smooth zipper operation around the corners.QuickCord for easy, no tie closure.ComfortCuff surrounds your face with softness.Commercial Machine washable.

REVIEW:
Not a good sleeping bag if you ask me. I purchased this at Walmart for about the same price as it is here and I've only used it once. After only one night a large (about 9 inches) section of the seams where it is stitched came apart. The temperature rating I would say is about right on. With the unfortunate seam opening I was able to see the filler material inside and I highly doubt it is cotton as it is described. Going to return it and look for a 100% cotton inside and out. For the life of me I don't know why anyone would want that sticky poly material against their skin in any way inside or out. It feels really gross. Spend a bit more and get a better bag.

data.title: Coleman Hooligan Backpacking Tent
data.description: The Hooligan 2 from Coleman is a 2 person backpacking tent. It features a full coverage coated polyester fly (450mm) that envelops the tent in a cocoon of protection from the harshest weather. A small front vestibule offers some storage space for extra gear and the all mesh body is great for ventilation and stargazing on nights when the weather is clear. The fly seams are sealed at the factory and the rugged 1000D polyethylene floor is a bathtub style (wraps up the sides) for extreme weather protection and has welded seams, even in the corners, to guard against leaks. Set up is with a single pole and thus could not be more simple and quick. Great for beginner backpackers, scouts and those who want to keep it simple.Non Freestanding (1 pole) design is simple to set up and take down.All mesh body for maximum ventilation and comfort and reduces condensation.Zippered back vent in the fly helps to keep the tent comfortable - adjustable through zipper on the back tent wall.Sturdy 11mm (1ea) Shockcorded fiberglass frame for easy set up.All mesh body is perfect for stargazing on nice nights.Full coverage fly is perfect for harsh weather.1 Small storage vestibule - 6 sq. ft. - good for boots etc..1 interior storage pocket.Easy convenient continuous pole sleeve construction.Pin and ring pole to body connection is fast and simple.External guy points to keep the tent stable in storms and high winds.Instructions sewn to carry bag so they are easy to find and hard to lose.All carry sacks and stakes included.Includes convenient flashlight loop on the ceiling.Fire retardant.Import..
data.reviewText: Nice tent. Survived it's first few nights in spite of some pretty heavy rain. The vestibule is much smaller that it appears in the picture. The picture in my opinion shows the vestibule to be almost the same footprint as the tent, which would have been nice.
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman Hooligan Backpacking Tent

DESCRIPTION:
The Hooligan 2 from Coleman is a 2 person backpacking tent. It features a full coverage coated polyester fly (450mm) that envelops the tent in a cocoon of protection from the harshest weather. A small front vestibule offers some storage space for extra gear and the all mesh body is great for ventilation and stargazing on nights when the weather is clear. The fly seams are sealed at the factory and the rugged 1000D polyethylene floor is a bathtub style (wraps up the sides) for extreme weather protection and has welded seams, even in the corners, to guard against leaks. Set up is with a single pole and thus could not be more simple and quick. Great for beginner backpackers, scouts and those who want to keep it simple.Non Freestanding (1 pole) design is simple to set up and take down.All mesh body for maximum ventilation and comfort and reduces condensation.Zippered back vent in the fly helps to keep the tent comfortable - adjustable through zipper on the back tent wall.Sturdy 11mm (1ea) Shockcorded fiberglass frame for easy set up.All mesh body is perfect for stargazing on nice nights.Full coverage fly is perfect for harsh weather.1 Small storage vestibule - 6 sq. ft. - good for boots etc..1 interior storage pocket.Easy convenient continuous pole sleeve construction.Pin and ring pole to body connection is fast and simple.External guy points to keep the tent stable in storms and high winds.Instructions sewn to carry bag so they are easy to find and hard to lose.All carry sacks and stakes included.Includes convenient flashlight loop on the ceiling.Fire retardant.Import..

REVIEW:
Nice tent. Survived it's first few nights in spite of some pretty heavy rain. The vestibule is much smaller that it appears in the picture. The picture in my opinion shows the vestibule to be almost the same footprint as the tent, which would have been nice.

data.title: Coleman Cabin Tent with Instant Setup | Cabin Tent for Camping Sets Up in 60 Seconds
data.description: The Coleman Instant Cabin makes getting camp set up quicker and easier than ever. This instant tent has pre-attached poles that make setting up camp as simple as unfolding, extending, and securing. You can assemble the tent in about a minute and then get started on your adventure. This camping tent is designed with the WeatherTec system that features patented welded floors and inverted seams to help ensure you stay dry if it starts to rain. An integrated vented rainfly offers added protection from the rain and improves airflow without the need for extra assembly. This backpacking tent is made from rugged Polyguard 2X double-thick fabric that stands up to the rigors of the outdoors, so you can use it reliably season after season. This 6-person tent offers enough room for two queen size air beds and comes with integrated storage pockets to help you keep small items organized. And when its time to go home, the camping tent packs away securely in the included expandable carry bag that lets you tear away the rip strip for easy packing.
data.reviewText:
I live in "Tornado Alley" in the Midwest, Kansas City area. If you don't know, we get some of the worst possible thunderstorms here that you could possibly imagine. 80+ mph winds, torrential downpours, low and VERY LOUD thunder and lightening. It feels like the roof of your house is going to blow off, as it creaks and moans the entire night during the storm.

We had one of these storms this past weekend, and I was camping in it with two of my little girls. And of course, I had my Coleman Instant Tent on it's maiden voyage. I knew we'd have bad weather, so I set a heavy duty tarp under the tent, partially to keep the water away from the tent, but also to protect the tent floor from wear. It was a typical hot and sticky midwest summer evening. Then about 2 AM, the winds came howling in at gale forces of 50 mph (according to the weather service), followed by EXTREMELY loud and heavy rain and thunder. The wind was blowing so crazily that it was hitting the tent from every different direction. Not one square inch of the tent exterior had any chance of staying dry. The rain and thunder continued for the next 5 or so hours with continued moments of VERY heavy rain and winds.

I am very pleased to say that we stayed totally dry and the tent incurred no damage from the storm. The also brand new tent (of a different Coleman model) of my friend who was next to us caved in on him and his kids at some point during the night (although he did stay dry). This Instant Tent turned out to be a great investment.

PROS
1) Easy setup... almost too easy. ;)

2) This tent weathers the storm!!! Remember, there is no fly on this tent, which is a great convenience for setup. But Coleman makes up for it with it's great seams and stronger material on this tent.

3) Very spacious with lots of head room.

4) Strong, well stitched material.

5) I like to take my tents home and put them up again so they will fully air dry before putting them away. The setup of this tent is so easy that it makes this very convenient.

6) It's much harder to lose parts on a tent that is already put together.

CONS
1) I've been involved in the Boy Scout program for decades and I've used my share of tents. This tent is sturdy, however, I can see how certain facets of the construction might not last through heavy regular use. For example the extension of the poles causes some wear on the painted surface which could cause rust. Certain critical parts of the poles and pop-up mechanism are plastic which I could see being points of failure after usage in several heavy storms or really cold weather. I can even see kids not caring for the mechanism as they should during setup and take down. However, don't let this deter you from this tent. The tent needs to be made of lighter parts in order to provide the convenience that makes this tent attractive. However, if you are planning on using this as your primary tent and/or use it quite often, keep this in mind. In my particular case, I have several tents, but wanted one that was spacious and easy to setup for quick overnight trips... This tent is perfect.

2) The bag that holds this thing is HUGE. I figure it's about twice the size (lengthwise) of a regular equivalent sized tent. This is expected because of the heavier material and the way the poles need to be constructed for the pop-up mechanism to work. This isn't a terrible thing, just don't expect it to be a small bag.

I would buy this tent again.
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman Cabin Tent with Instant Setup | Cabin Tent for Camping Sets Up in 60 Seconds

DESCRIPTION:
The Coleman Instant Cabin makes getting camp set up quicker and easier than ever. This instant tent has pre-attached poles that make setting up camp as simple as unfolding, extending, and securing. You can assemble the tent in about a minute and then get started on your adventure. This camping tent is designed with the WeatherTec system that features patented welded floors and inverted seams to help ensure you stay dry if it starts to rain. An integrated vented rainfly offers added protection from the rain and improves airflow without the need for extra assembly. This backpacking tent is made from rugged Polyguard 2X double-thick fabric that stands up to the rigors of the outdoors, so you can use it reliably season after season. This 6-person tent offers enough room for two queen size air beds and comes with integrated storage pockets to help you keep small items organized. And when its time to go home, the camping tent packs away securely in the included expandable carry bag that lets you tear away the rip strip for easy packing.

REVIEW:
I live in "Tornado Alley" in the Midwest, Kansas City area. If you don't know, we get some of the worst possible thunderstorms here that you could possibly imagine. 80+ mph winds, torrential downpours, low and VERY LOUD thunder and lightening. We had one of these storms this past weekend, and I was camping in it with two of my little girls. And of course, I had my Coleman Instant Tent on it's maiden voyage. I knew we'd have bad weather, so I set a heavy duty tarp under the tent, partially to keep the water away from the tent, but also to protect the tent floor from wear. It was a typical hot and sticky midwest summer evening. Then about 2 AM, the winds came howling in at gale forces of 50 mph (according to the weather service), followed by EXTREMELY loud and heavy rain and thunder. The wind was blowing so crazily that it was hitting the tent from every different direction. Not one square inch of the tent exterior had any chance of staying dry. The rain and thunder continued for the next 5 or so hours with continued moments of VERY heavy rain and winds.

I am very pleased to say that we stayed totally dry and the tent incurred no damage from the storm. The also brand new tent (of a different Coleman model) of my friend who was next to us caved in on him and his kids at some point during the night (although he did stay dry). This Instant Tent turned out to be a great investment.

PROS
1) Easy setup... almost too easy. ;)

2) This tent weathers the storm!!! Remember, there is no fly on this tent, which is a great convenience for setup. But Coleman makes up for it with it's great seams and stronger material on this tent.

3) Very spacious with lots of head room.

4) Strong, well stitched material.

5) I like to take my tents home and put them up again so they will fully air dry before putting them away. The setup of this tent is so easy that it makes this very convenient.

6) It's much harder to lose parts on a tent that is already put together.

CONS
1) I've been involved in the Boy Scout program for decades and I've used my share of tents. This tent is sturdy, however, I can see how certain facets of the construction might not last through heavy regular use. For example the extension of the poles causes some wear on the painted surface which could cause rust. Certain critical parts of the poles and pop-up mechanism are plastic which I could see being points of failure after usage in several heavy storms or really cold weather. I can even see kids not caring for the mechanism as they should during setup and take down. However, don't let this deter you from this tent. The tent needs to be made of lighter parts in order to provide the convenience that makes this tent attractive. However, if you are planning on using this as your primary tent and/or use it quite often, keep this in mind. In my particular case, I have several tents, but wanted one that was spacious and easy to setup for quick overnight trips... This tent is perfect.

2) The bag that holds this thing is HUGE. I figure it's about twice the size (lengthwise) of a regular equivalent sized tent. This is expected because of the heavier material and the way the poles need to be constructed for the pop-up mechanism to work. This isn't a terrible thing, just don't expect it to be a small bag.

I would buy this tent again.

data.title: Coleman 16-Can Soft Cooler with Removable Liner
data.description: The Coleman 16-Can Soft Cooler goes with you to picnics, BBQs, camping, sporting events, tailgates and beyond. It holds 16 cans inside, and features plenty of extra storage space in its front zippered pocket, side mesh pockets, and mesh pocket in the lid. There are even bungees on the lid for stowing more extras! Sporting an adjustable shoulder strap, the cooler is soft on the outside for comfortable carrying, and has a hard plastic liner inside to protect your food from being crushed. The liner material is FDA approved for food contact, and is removable for easy cleaning. The Soft Cooler provides antimicrobial protection, to resist odors, mold, and mildew.
data.reviewText: I really like this bag except for one problem, it really is a pain to carry. I didn't think about the strap and not having a handle. It is very awkward trying to carry it, if this had a handle on the top (the way men would carry it) this would be five stars. I don't want to wear it as a purse and the strap is too long and wide to use as a carry handle. I end up carrying it by the bungee straps and it bounces the whole time. One more thing about this that bugs me is the zipper, why do they build this so the top section lining sticks out when unzipped, I have to shove it back in when trying to rezip it closed. Besides those issues I really do like it, I love the size, it can fit everything I need with no problem.
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman 16-Can Soft Cooler with Removable Liner

DESCRIPTION:
The Coleman 16-Can Soft Cooler goes with you to picnics, BBQs, camping, sporting events, tailgates and beyond. It holds 16 cans inside, and features plenty of extra storage space in its front zippered pocket, side mesh pockets, and mesh pocket in the lid. There are even bungees on the lid for stowing more extras! Sporting an adjustable shoulder strap, the cooler is soft on the outside for comfortable carrying, and has a hard plastic liner inside to protect your food from being crushed. The liner material is FDA approved for food contact, and is removable for easy cleaning. The Soft Cooler provides antimicrobial protection, to resist odors, mold, and mildew.

REVIEW:
I really like this bag except for one problem, it really is a pain to carry. I didn't think about the strap and not having a handle. It is very awkward trying to carry it, if this had a handle on the top (the way men would carry it) this would be five stars. I don't want to wear it as a purse and the strap is too long and wide to use as a carry handle. I end up carrying it by the bungee straps and it bounces the whole time. One more thing about this that bugs me is the zipper, why do they build this so the top section lining sticks out when unzipped, I have to shove it back in when trying to rezip it closed. Besides those issues I really do like it, I love the size, it can fit everything I need with no problem.

data.title: Coleman Mens Apparel 10mm Pvc Rain Suit Advantage Max-4
data.description: Light Weight east to carry and put on when the weather changes quickly. Plenty of ventalation and adjustability.
data.reviewText: Glad you have it in your pack if you get caught in an unexpected rain or snow..Packs down and takes up very little room..It holds up well in most situations, but if you have to go through any briers, they shred like paper! Very noisy if your trying to hunt...But all that said, if you want something cheap to keep in your pack for unexpected weather and don't care to throw away after a couple uses, these fit the bill. The suit does a good job keeping you dry if you are sitting on stand or walking where there is nothing for it to snag on. For the price, and small size of the package, I keep one in my pack...May not last but better than getting soaking wet!

data.title: Coleman Dome Tent for Camping | Sundome Tent with Easy Setup
data.description: Sundome 2P Dome TentFeatures:Up to 33% more water resistant verses a comparable standard Coleman tent Weathertec System patented welded floors and inverted seams help keep water outSnag-Free continuous pole sleeves for easy setup Ground Vent for increased ventilationTwo windows for great ventilation and viewsDoor awning helps keep the rain out E-Port makes it easy to bring electrical power inside your tent Storage pocket to keep your gear organized Expandable carry bag lets you tear away the rip strip for easy packingSpecifications:Doors:1 D-DoorRainfly:PolyguardTent:Polyguard,Polyester Mesh 68DPoles:FiberglassWeight: 7.2 (3.3 KG)<br/
data.reviewText: This tent was a beast. It's too small for two people to be honest. Tall people might find it a wee bit claustrophobic so you might want to splurge on the 3 person version. But it fit my cot, my gear, and me -  and withstood 50 mph winds at the campsite. I did have to take the rainfly off however because it made the tent more airborne. It rattled like a ship's sail all night, but stood up to whatever the Mojave blew at it. I may get a second one to store in case I destroy this one. Get better tent pegs and a mallet though. The ones supplied were crap and bent and I had to bring a hammer from my kit at home. There were two tents better than mine and they were much more expensive. Suck it Mojave Desert!

data.title: Coleman 8-Person Tent for Camping | Red Canyon Car Camping Tent
data.description: Measuring 17- by 10-feet, Coleman's Red Canyon tent is spacious enough to comfortably sleep eight campers. Ideal for weekend camping trips with family and friends, the Red Canyon features removable privacy walls, allowing you to create up to three separate rooms.
data.reviewText:
Nice size tent and sleeps 8 people tightly.
No complaints until a storm came up. Graphite tent poles NOT rated for 40 mph winds.
Some of the smaller tent poles broke. Wish they were made of aluminum like my old Coleman tent was they could withstand the elements. Not bad for the $ but has been a pain since the storm hit.  Oh well....

data.title: Coleman Evanston Tent
data.description: Enjoy the fresh air without the bugs when you're camping in a Coleman Evanston Tent. It's designed for simple setup and easy carryingperfect for car campers and long camping trips. The tent even has a screened porch where you can sleep on warm, dry nights. If it rains, the tent is designed to hold up to the weather.
data.reviewText:
We have owned this tent for several years.  It has been used for family camping and girl scout camping when I want to put half a dozen girl scouts in the same tent.

The only problem we've had with it was when we went to Monahans State Park (Texas), and the wind was really high one night.  The tent couldn't handle the wind, and the high sides that make it wonderful because you can stand up in it also turn it into a big sail in high winds.  We ended up purposely collapsing the tent before a pole had a chance to break.

I've found that it's easy to put up once you get the hang of it (I can put it up by myself in about 10 minutes).  Putting the rain fly on without a helper is much more difficult just because you have to get it over the top.

For those asking about size, I'm attaching a few pictures - I was checking equipment before a girl scout campout, so I had two queen air mattresses and a twin blown up inside the tent earlier.  You can see that there's plenty of room for those plus a lot more for any gear they bring along.

Two propane pouches with drawstrings securely carry extra fuel canisters (sold separately).

data.title: Coleman Propane Grill | RoadTrip LXE Portable Gas Grill
data.description: Stop manhandling those heavy, bulky backyard beasts and show off as the grill master with the Coleman RoadTrip LXE Propane Grill. It's easy to tote around and set up. Just fold it up, roll it with one hand to your favorite cooking spot and set it up in a matter of seconds with the lift and lock system. The grill comes equipped with durable cast-iron grilling surfaces. (Coleman RoadTrip pot support and griddles are available separately.) When you're ready to grill, push a button to ignite the InstaStart matchless lighting system. Cook for more than an hour on one propane cylinder with 20,000 BTUs of power on 285 sq. in. (1838.7 sq. cm) of cooking surface. It's perfect for BBQs, picnics, tailgating, camping or a night on the patio. When you're ready to leave, it folds down small enough to fit in the back of most car trunks.

data.title: Coleman 16-Quart Personal Wheeled Cooler, Blue
data.description:
Keep your food and drinks cold, and keep the fun going, for up to 2 days when you roll in with a Coleman 16 Quart Performance Wheeled Cooler. Large enough to hold 22 cans, you'll have plenty of space for refreshments for several people. Whether you're headed on an overnight camping trip, to a tailgate party, or across the ball field, the recessed wheels and long, telescoping handle let you roll up with hardly any effort. The additional molded handles make it easy to lift your cooler in and out of the car or onto a picnic bench. Coleman provides a convenient way to keep things cold, even when you're on-the-go, with the 16-Quart Personal Wheeled Cooler. This convenient cooler can hold up to 22 12-ounce cans or two 2-liter bottles upright and features traditional Coleman construction for rugged durability. Other features include an extendable handle that retracts for convenient storage and recessed wheels for easy transport to and from your favorite sporting events and outdoor activities. This durable Coleman cooler comes with a limited manufacturer's warranty. About Coleman
The Coleman Company has been creating and innovating products for recreational outdoor use since W.C. Coleman started selling gasoline-powered lanterns in 1900. Inventor of the hugely popular fold-up camp stove, Coleman developed a plastic liner for his galvanized steel coolers in 1957--the birth of the modern cooler--and the company has been improving their utility and design ever since. The array of products that bear the Coleman name now includes just about everything you might need to work or play outdoors, from tents and sleeping bags to boats, backpacks, and furniture.
data.reviewText: The cooler was OK but it was smaller than advertised so I returned it and ordered the larger size 28 qt Igloo cooler.

data.title: Coleman Cooler Standard Drain Plug Assembly - 1" Shaft Length
data.reviewText:
I needed these replacement drain parts for work coolers.  We have a wide variety of sizes and types but most are Coleman brand coolers.  We tend to ship a lot of coolers and when we get them back they tend to be broken either by the delivery company or the lab that we shipped to.  We would always just start stripping the smaller coolers for parts to use on the larger coolers because they are needed more often and are more expensive to replace but after a while we had cooler lids and bases and did not want to spend the money on replacement coolers.

Finding these drain replacement parts was the last step in putting some of our coolers back together.  You can also purchase the handles and hinges on Amazon.  These parts are the exact same pieces that come with the whole coolers and fit perfectly.  Being an add-on item they only cost 4.99 a piece when purchased with another item but if you need a few drain replacement parts add enough to be over 25 dollars which is 6 of them.  These coolers will last and last with these replacement parts.  If we need more I will definitely buy here again.

We tend to ship a lot of coolers and when we get them back they tend to be broken either by the delivery company or the lab that we shipped to.  We would always just start stripping the smaller coolers for parts to use on the larger coolers because they are needed more often and are more expensive to replace but after a while we had cooler lids and bases and did not want to spend the money on replacement coolers.

Finding these drain replacement parts was the last step in putting some of our coolers back together.  You can also purchase the handles and hinges on Amazon.  These parts are the exact same pieces that come with the whole coolers and fit perfectly.  Being an add-on item they only cost 4.99 a piece when purchased with another item but if you need a few drain replacement parts add enough to be over 25 dollars which is 6 of them.  These coolers will last and last with these replacement parts.  If we need more I will definitely buy here again.

data.title: Coleman Camping Instant Signal Mountain Tent
data.reviewText:
I am an avid tent camper, having camped in wilderness conditions with temperatures as low as 17 degrees F up to 110 degrees (at night). Ive gone through more tents than I can remember and there are things Ive loved about some of them and other things Ive disliked. This is the second instant tent Ive owned and resisted buying it exactly for that feature  the first one was too delicate and didn't even last a season.

Starting at the very beginning, the absolutely essential thing a person should do upon receiving a new tent is to set it up in their backyard (or other appropriate place if an apartment dweller) and be sure all of the parts are there, that they fit, that all zippers work and that there are no obvious defects.  The second thing?  And before that first camping trip, spray a garden hose at your new tent to be assured that it is reasonably water-tight.

Passing that test, you can approach your first camping trip with a fair degree of confidence.  My issue of this tent passed perfectly.

Heres what I like about this tent, especially as compared to other tents I have owned:

1. The fabric is a bit heavier than most tents in the sub-$300 price range. You can feel the thickness.
2. The zippers are stronger feeling and being D shaped they don't have any convoluted twists and turns.
3. The tent does not need a separate storm/rain cover. The second night we were camping it rained most of the night and the inside of the tent was bone dry.
4. It does set up about as fast as Coleman says it will. I set it up the first time by myself and it took about 3 minutes. With another person it truly can be erected in just over a minute.
5. The high-wind performance is phenomenal. Our first night ever using the tent the winds averaged 20 MPH with gusts close to 40 MPH and higher just after sunrise. The tent bucked and bowed and swayed and felt like a thrill ride at a theme park but with only 6 cheap stakes it held firm. It flexed like crazy but never lost its footing.
6. The walls are almost vertical with very high ceilings.  Yes! Finally a tent I can stand up in and walk around. During the peak of the rain we were able to sit in the living room in our camp chairs with plenty of room to relax.

Here are some observations:

1. I love that the bedroom can be closed off to help keep it dark and the living room can be almost totally opened up with screened windows for the maximum view. But I have mixed feelings that there is no skylight at all  I love to fall asleep gazing up at the stars and this tent does not make that possible.
2. For a tent this large there aren't enough pockets for personal belongings (in the bedroom) and no pass-throughs for shoes and such.
3. At 23 lbsthis tent is big and heavy. I cant fault Coleman for that, there would be no way to make a tent this large with the extra mechanisms to be self-erecting and make it light.

A good tent can cost $500 - $600 (or more) and frankly it does not cost that much to make a tent. At about $190 when reviewed it is a LOT of tent for the price and well worth the money. Its not quite 5-stars because it lacks a few things that a company like Coleman would have been expected to provide and what could be a deal breaker for some people who camp a lot.  But its wind/rain protection are admirable and the quick setup and teardown really make handling such a large tent much more pleasant. Overall, at the selling price it may not be a lifetime tent but it is a very good value and the quality of materials is marginally better than most other tents Ive seen anywhere near the price.

data.title: Coleman Portable Butane Stove with Carrying Case
data.description: On-the-go cooking just got a little easier with the Coleman Portable Butane Stove with Carrying Case. With an ultra-portable design and included carry case, this stove is ready for any camping adventure and quickly sets up on any outdoor tabletop, supporting up to a 10-inch pan. Attach an 8.8 oz. butane cylinder (sold separately), light the automatic matchless ignition with the turn of a knob, and in no time, youre cooking with up to 7,650 BTUs of power for up to 1.25 hours. Wind baffles shield your flame from the wind, and a large stable base makes stirring easy. Use your stove for years to come thanks to an easy-to-clean, durable porcelain-coated grate, rust-proof aluminum burner, and made-to-last enameled steel exterior.
data.reviewText: Love it..!

data.title: Coleman Camp Axe
data.description: Get the right tool for multiple jobs when you're at the campsite with the Coleman Camp Axe. The sharp edge of the durable drop-forged steel head easily splits wood for a campfire or stove, while the flat edge works wonders for driving tent stakes into hard ground. When it's time to pack up, pulling your tent stakes is easy with the notch cut into the head. Durable and well built, the Coleman Camp Axe is perfect for the campsite, wooded terrain, or the backyard. The axe is equipped with a drop-forged carbon-steel axe head that easily splits wood into kindling, along with a forged steel handle and an ergonomic nonslip grip. With the Coleman Camp Axe, you'll be ready for any camping contingency.
data.reviewText: I bought a Coleman Camp Axe a year or so ago for camping and for mostly breaking down firewood into kindling. I bought it because it was cheap and had decent reviews. Overall that axe seemed fine, and for the price I suppose it is. However, I had to replace it. The cons with the Coleman are that it seems as though the steel is soft--it shows major dents from hitting who knows what--and most importantly it doesn't come with any realistic sheath to protect the blade. Sheaths on Amazon will double the price of this, if not more. Based upon the damage to the Coleman and wanting a sheath to protect the blade I ended up buying the Gerber 31-000914 Camp Axe II High Performance Axe. I've used it now on one camping trip, and I have to say I love it. It seems to be an almost perfect tool. No comparison to the Coleman at all.

data.title: Coleman 8-Person Tent for Camping | Red Canyon Car Camping Tent
data.description: Measuring 17- by 10-feet, Coleman's Red Canyon tent is spacious enough to comfortably sleep eight campers. Ideal for weekend camping trips with family and friends, the Red Canyon features removable privacy walls, allowing you to create up to three separate rooms.
data.reviewText: Plenty of room fit 3 full size air mattress' 6 people and 1 dog very comfortably with room to spare. I would have given this product more stars however one of the seams did leak some water inside. Seam protector is needed

data.title: Coleman 9.5-In.Frying Pan
data.description: Fry up an incredible meal in the convenience of the Coleman 9.5-In. Frying Pan. Its non-stick coating makes outdoor cooking and cleaning easy. It even comes with a vinyl-coated cool grip handle that folds over the pan for compact storage.
data.reviewText:
This pan is a fine example of JUNK.
I tried to cook an egg on the one we had. That was the end of the pan.  Even with a bit of oil the egg never came off the pan.
A big disappointment coming from Coleman.

data.title: Coleman Camping Coffee Maker
data.description: Easily make drip-brewed coffee while enjoying the outdoors with the Coleman Camping Coffee Maker. Designed with a steel base that fits over most 2- and 3-burner camp stoves, this camping coffee maker features a large reservoir that enables you to brew up to 10 cups of hot coffee at once. Making coffee with this outdoor coffee maker is easy. Simply spoon grounds into the removable swing-out filter basket, fill the reservoir with water, and place the coffee maker over your stove burner. Within minutes, the 10-cup glass carafe will be filled with freshly brewed coffee ready to be enjoyed in the great outdoors. A Pause 'N Serve feature lets you grab a cup before the brew cycle is finished. The Coleman Camping Coffee Maker measures 8.5 x 11 x 14 inches. About Coleman More than 100 years ago, a young man with an entrepreneurial spirit and a better idea began manufacturing lanterns in Wichita, Kansas. His name was W.C. Coleman, and the company he founded would change life in America. A man plagued with such poor vision he sometimes had to ask classmates to read aloud to him, Coleman saw a brilliant light in 1900 in a drugstore window that stopped him in his tracks. He inquired about the light inside and discovered he was able to read even the small print on a medicine bottle by the illumination. The lamps had mantles, not wicks, and were fueled by gasoline under pressure instead of coal oil. Soon afterward, Coleman started a lighting service that offered a "no light, no pay" clause--a big step forward for merchants who were burned by inferior products that rarely worked--and drew substantial interest from businesses that wanted to keep their lights on after dark. In the ensuing years, Coleman expanded its product line well beyond lanterns. The company's current catalog is thick with products that make spending time outdoors a pleasure. There are coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days on end, comfortable airbeds that won't deflate during the night, a complete line of LED lights that last for years, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and much, much more. Coleman has truly fashioned much of our outdoor camping experience, and expects to do so for generations to come. END
data.reviewText: I have used this for 2 camping seasons (about 5 or 6 times total) and am not really that happy with this coffee maker. It is very slow to brew coffee and after brewing I usually only get 8 cups or so despite filling it up for 12 cups. On my last camping trip I had problems with the top popping off and the bottom actually had a good chunk of metal melt off. The coffee it makes is OK, but I think I will try something else the next trip. I am contacting Coleman for a refund or see if there has been recalls for the issues I have had.

I have used this for 2 camping seasons (about 5 or 6 times total) and am not really that happy with this coffee maker. It is very slow to brew coffee and after brewing I usually only get 8 cups or so despite filling it up for 12 cups. On my last camping trip I had problems with the top popping off and the bottom actually had a good chunk of metal melt off. The coffee it makes is OK, but I think I will try something else the next trip. I am contacting Coleman for a refund or see if there has been recalls for the issues I have had.

data.title: Coleman Sportster Propane Grill
data.description: Get your grilling game on with the Coleman Sportster Propane Grill. The collapsible, wheeled stand makes this grill easy to take wherever you need it. Once you're at your location, the lift and lock system makes it easy to securely set up. Hit the Instastart push-button ignition and get ready to cook with 11,000 total BTUs of grilling power. The 225-sq.-in. porcelain-coated stamped steel grate is large enough to cook up a family-sized meal. After dinner is done, cleaning is easy. A removable tray collects the grease while you're cooking, and the porcelain-coated grate is easy to remove to wipe it down. Then just latch the lid, collapse the legs, and you can be on your way to your next adventure.
data.reviewText:
One star off for the lack of instructions. And for not listening to the complaints and posting assembling on YouTube. There was a latch that was not visible on any of the pictures. Thanks to an 11-min YouTube video I was able to determine which side it went.  I couldn't believe that they didn't include a cheapie wrench that is essential for assembly.

Another star off because as I wheeled it outside it bumped very slightly on the corner of the couch and the wheel came off. The piece that you screw into is ab a 1/8" cap that is pushed on to the horizontal bar. There's no way it will stay on. Might need to be glued.

Another star off because the auto ignition didn't work at all. And that silly little chain with a match holder is pretty useless. Doesn't everybody use this long automatic lighters?  I'm only 5' (female) and I recommend having a chair close by. Especially since the canister receiver doesn't swivel (the one in my Camp Chef stove/oven does) and it was very awkward to screw on the canister.

So, I balanced it on a piece of wood, removed the grill so I could light it manually, realized I'd forgotten to insert the drip pan but couldn't slide it in from the front and it was already hot, and put the T-bone on.  It cooked fine. I can't be bothered to light coals for just one person.

Ain't no way no how that I'm going to dismantle it and go to all the trouble of trying to repackage it to return so I'll have to jury rig it. And I did want to be able to take it camping so we'll have to see.

Thanks to other reviewers I lined the bowl and the drip pan with foil, and ordered the Weber cover for the 1,000/2,000 series. I put the Weber logo to the back.

The YouTube reviewer mentioned that there is a newer version that has two burners. I paid $88 for mine about 10 days ago and it's now $106 but I glanced at the description and couldn't tell if it's the newer model. Probably as it's still available for $85 under other purchasing options.

To add to the cluster, I can't notify Amazon of the problems because although it arrived on Sunday (today is Wednesday) the order shows as being delayed and the option doesn't come up. I've never given such a low review but...

Postscript 5 days later:although the grill still appears as undelivered/delayed, I was able to
chat with a Customer Service rep. I included an edited/expanded version of my review.  When asked what I wanted I said "a wheel part that doesn't come off."

I was stunned and amazed when I was offered a replacement at no charge. I told him that I wasn't willing to dissemble the one I have plus I'd cut up and recycled both boxes (they are huge and I have no room) so difficult to return the original, I was told to keep the defective one and a replacement would be sent via one-day shipping!! This was Saturday and it arrived on Monday afternoon.

I'm preparing for an upcoming international trip and can't open (and assemble ) the new one right now (plus my recycle bin is full), but I didn't want to hold back my congratulations to Amazon for handling this situation over and above what I had expected.

data.title: Coleman 9-In. Heavy Duty Aluminum Tent Stakes
data.description: You'll Sleep soundly knowing your tent is secured with Coleman 9-in. Heavy duty aluminum tent stakes. These lightweight stakes are specially-designed to hold up to large tents and strong winds. When the fun is done, the lanyard on each tip makes easy work of pulling out the stake. Each package includes four stakes.
data.reviewText:
Did not survive one use in hard-pack gravel.  MSR brand aluminum stakes of a very similar "tri-blade" design did.

The Coleman ones mushroomed at the head, bent at the tie notch, and in one case split up the middle.  Very disappointed but would have written it off as "tough conditions are tough on gear" if the MSR ones had not survived with flying colors.
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman 9-In. Heavy Duty Aluminum Tent Stakes

DESCRIPTION:
You'll Sleep soundly knowing your tent is secured with Coleman 9-in. Heavy duty aluminum tent stakes. These lightweight stakes are specially-designed to hold up to large tents and strong winds. When the fun is done, the lanyard on each tip makes easy work of pulling out the stake. Each package includes four stakes.

REVIEW:
Did not survive one use in hard-pack gravel.  MSR brand aluminum stakes of a very similar "tri-blade" design did.

The Coleman ones mushroomed at the head, bent at the tie notch, and in one case split up the middle.  Very disappointed but would have written it off as "tough conditions are tough on gear" if the MSR ones had not survived with flying colors.

data.title: Coleman Portable Camping Quad Chair with 4-Can Cooler
data.description: Have a cold drink at hand at the campsite, BBQ or picnic with a Coleman Cooler Quad Chair. The cooler built into the armrest holds up to 4 cans and provides easy access to a cold drink once the one in your mesh cup holder is empty. While you sip on your favorite beverage, the fully cushioned seat and back only adds to your relaxation. If you want your hands free, the side pockets work nicely to store your book, magazine or some snacks. When the fun is done, the chair easily folds up into a carry bag for easy transport and storage.
data.reviewText: first time I used it collapsed. Im no near 325 lbs. Unfortunately its past its return date. By the ratings I think this was a fluke but I won't be buying another.

His name was W.C. Coleman, and the company he founded would change life in America. A man plagued with such poor vision he sometimes had to ask classmates to read aloud to him, Coleman saw a brilliant light in 1900 in a drugstore window that stopped him in his tracks. He inquired about the light inside and discovered he was able to read even the small print on a medicine bottle by the illumination. The lamps had mantles, not wicks, and were fueled by gasoline under pressure instead of coal oil. Soon afterward, Coleman started a lighting service that offered a "no light, no pay" clause--a big step forward for merchants who were burned by inferior products that rarely worked--and drew substantial interest from businesses that wanted to keep their lights on after dark. In the ensuing years, Coleman expanded its product line well beyond lanterns. The company's current catalog is thick with products that make spending time outdoors a pleasure. There are coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days on end, comfortable airbeds that won't deflate during the night, a complete line of LED lights that last for years, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and much, much more. data.reviewText: Right out of the box this thing is defective. One of the wind shields fell off when opening it. Upon inspection, the one that's still attached is held on very poorly. You'd think they'd have a better fastener to hold on the wind shields, but they don't. I guess that's what we should expect from Coleman since they have their products made in China, which makes them pieces of junk. I received this 15 minutes ago, and I already initiated the return for a refund. What a joke!

data.title: Coleman Steel-Belted Portable Cooler, 54 Quart
data.description: Go back in time with cold food and drinks today when you take a Coleman 54 Quart Steel Belted Cooler along with you. It looks just like the one you remember with its classic design first developed in 1954. Large enough to hold 85 cans and tall enough to hold 2-liter bottles upright, you'll have plenty of space for refreshments for a whole group of people. Whether you're headed on an overnight camping trip, to a tailgate party, or out to a BBQ, the comfort-grip steel handles and stainless steel lid latch make getting to your destination easy, comfortable and secure. Once you arrive, the Have-A-Seat lid will ensure you always have a place to sit and relax while you enjoy the day with a cold drink in hand. When the fun is done, the leak-resistant channel drain makes it easy to empty any excess water without tilting the cooler, so you're ready for your next fun adventure. A Coleman Steel Belted Cooler will rekindle your fondest outdoor memories  and equip you to make many new ones.
data.reviewText: The quality isn't great. I had to return the first one and the second one they sent me has bubbles in the finish. Too much trouble to return it again.

The lamps had mantles, not wicks, and were fueled by gasoline under pressure instead of coal oil. Soon afterward, Coleman started a lighting service that offered a "no light, no pay" clause--a big step forward for merchants who were burned by inferior products that rarely worked--and drew substantial interest from businesses that wanted to keep their lights on after dark. In the ensuing years, Coleman expanded its product line well beyond lanterns. The company's current catalog is thick with products that make spending time outdoors a pleasure. There are coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days on end, comfortable airbeds that won't deflate during the night, a complete line of LED lights that last for years, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and much, much more. Coleman has truly fashioned much of our outdoor camping experience, and expects to do so for generations to come.

REVIEW:
Classic design that can't be beat (at least at this price).

data.title: Coleman Bottle Top Propane Stove,Green,6.62" H x 7.81" W x 7.75" L
data.description:
Need a lightweight outdoor stove for backpacking, hunting, or camping? Look no further than the Coleman Portable Bottle-Top Propane Stove, which fits up to an 8-inch pan above a fully adjustable burner for precise temperature control and up to 10,000 BTUs of cooking power. Brew coffee, heat up hot dogs, and so on, knowing you can enjoy up to 2.5 hours of cooking on just one 16.4 oz. propane cylinder (sold separately). PerfectFlow technology keeps that heat steady, even in extreme conditions, and a large base offers stability for easy stirring. When its time to break down the campsite and fit all your gear into a crowded pack, youll be relieved to know that the burner and base separate from the propane cylinder for truly compact packing. Rest assured your ultra-portable stove comes with a 3-year limited warranty. The Coleman 5431A700 One-burner Propane Stove is an easy-to-use portable stove that should meet almost any camp cooking need. The PerfectFlow regulator provides consistent cooking performance by producing a steady fuel stream, even in cold weather, high altitudes, or when fuel is low. Equipped with one 10,000 BTU burner, this fully adjustable stove will last for 2.2 hours on high or up to nine hours on low. The pot supports help shield your cooking flame from the wind, while the large eight-inch burner bowl should fit most any pot. This Coleman one-burner stove can boil a quart of water in just four minutes! This stove operates on one 16.4-ounce cylinder of Coleman propane (not included). Manufacturer Warranty
Limited lifetime warranty
data.reviewText:
I was really hoping to give this a good review because I love Coleman, but the stove is small, heavy, not easy to compact carry, and it leaks like crazy.

Even at absolute off the stove will still leak a small but steady flame if there is no wind. I blame it on the design of the knob being so easily exposed and fairly cheaply made. The rest of the stove could hold a damn picnic table, but the control knob is super cheap plastic in the wrong place.

I have only taken this camping once, did not abuse it, and it was brand new. About the 3rd time I used it I noticed the gas leak.

This seems like a great idea as a backup, stay-at-home just in case type stove, but it is not light enough for hiking and it leaks so bad that you wouldn't think to leave on your propane tank ever.

That said, it is a good working stove that can make a single propane tank last quite a while

data.title: Coleman LED Camp Lantern
data.description: Light up the night with a Coleman 4AA LED Camp Lantern. Five bright white LEDs produce 54 lumens of effective, efficient light output that you can take with you wherever you go outside and indoors. The lantern boasts a long runtime of on four AA-cell batteries, so the sun will come up long before you need fresh power. The lightweight, durable, weather-resistant construction will withstand the elements, for illumination that keeps up with you, whatever the adventure.
data.reviewText: So small. I must have overlooked that fact when researching for a decent camp table sized lantern. What an embarrassment. Can be used inside a small tent at best.

data.title: Coleman 8D Family Size LED Lantern
data.description:
The Coleman 8D Family-Size LED Lantern is a strong, reliable light source for camping, fishing, travel, and as a standby for power outages and other emergencies. The bright, long-lasting Cree XR-E LED provides 170 lumens on the "high" setting. The innovative conical reflector diffuses and disseminates light for a wide, spotlight-like area of illumination. The lantern runs for a full 66 hours on low and 32 hours on high, with a set of 8 D-cell batteries (not included). It even sports a "nightlight" setting. The LED Lantern is weather-resistant, with durable ABS housing, a snug fit clear globe, chrome-plated globe guards, and an overlapping ventilator top for duty in the great outdoors. A bail handle makes the lantern easy to hang up, carry, pack and store. The Coleman 8D Family-Size LED Lantern provides a strong and reliable light source for camping, fishing, travel, and as a standby for power outages and other emergencies. The bright, long-lasting Cree XR-E LED provides 170 lumens on the "high" setting and will not heat up under extended use, making it a safe option for families. The light is equipped with an innovative conical reflector that diffuses and disseminates light for a wide, spotlight-like area of illumination. With three settings, low, high, and nightlight, the lantern offers impressive runtimes of 66 hours on low and 32 hours on high, with a set of 8 D-cell batteries (not included). Designed for all-purpose and outdoors use, the lantern boasts a durable weather-resistant ABS housing, a snug fit clear globe, chrome-plated rhino guards, and an overlapping ventilator top. A bail handle makes the lantern easy to hang up, carry, pack, and store. Features and Specifications:
  • Light settings: high; low; nightlight
  • Maximum output: 175 lumens (high)
  • Run times: 66 hours on low; 32 hours on high
  • Four-position switch: Off/High/Low/Nightlight
  • Durable chrome-plated rhino guard
  • Weather resistant ABS housing
  • Snug fit clear globe
  • Bail handle
  • Cool touch LED
  • Weight: 3.26 pounds without batteries
  • Dimensions: 11.81 by 6.53 to 7.28 inches (HxW)
  • Operates: 8D batteries (sold separately)
  • Made in China
data.reviewText: I refuse to by cheap Chinese made Coleman products

data.title: Coleman 9-Can Soft Cooler with Removable Liner
data.description: Keep food and drinks cold with the Coleman Soft Cooler. This compact, portable cooler bag holds up to nine cans and offers plenty of space for a family picnic or game-day snacks. Heat-welded seams in the zippered main compartment prevent leaks and maintain a cold environment, while the removable hard plastic liner fits snugly inside the soft cooler and allows you to easily dump out any ice melt. When not using the hard plastic liner, you can use ice substitutes to keep contents cold and dry. The cooler bag's easy-clean interior has an antimicrobial lining to resist formation of mold, mildew, fungus, and odor. A zippered front pocket, pocket under the lid, and two side mesh pockets make it easy to carry water bottles, snacks, silverware, and other accessories. You can also secure items to the top with the convenient bungee cords built into the lid. An adjustable shoulder strap lets you comfortably carry the cooler anywhere you go.
data.reviewText: The bag wasn't as handy and was way too bulky for the train commute i had to make daily so

data.title: Coleman Dome Tent for Camping | Sundome Tent with Easy Setup
data.description: The Coleman Sundome 6, 10'x10', dome tent is part of the classic Sundome series of tents from Coleman. The Sundome 6 sleeps 6 happy campers and includes a large door for easy entry/exit with a hooded fly to provide ventilation even in rain. A large rear hooded window also provides airflow for cross ventilation. Mesh vents on the roof provide additional ventilation and the rugged 1000D polyethylene floor is a bathtub style (wraps up the sides) for extreme weather protection and has welded seams, even in the corners, to guard against a leaky floor. The coated polyester fabrics on the fly and walls are rugged and durable. Like all Coleman Sundome tents it is easy to set up or take down and features an Electrical Access Port, a ground vent and an interior mesh storage pocket.Freestanding design is easy to set up and take down.2 windows and roof vents for maximum ventilation and comfort.Mesh roof aids in ventilation and reduces condensation.Shockcorded fiberglass frame for easy set up.Polyester fly is tough and durable and won't stretch when wet.1 zippered Electrical Access Port to run AC power into the tent.1 vented Cool Air Port for increased ventilation and comfort.1 interior storage pocket.Easy convenient clip and continuous pole sleeve construction.Pin and ring pole to body connection is fast and simple.External guy points to keep the tent stable in storms and high winds.Instructions sewn to carry bag so they are easy to find and hard to lose.All carry sacks and stakes included.Includes convenient flashlight loop on the ceiling.Fire retardant.Import.
data.reviewText:
the tent has some nice features. The only bad thing is lack of ventilation with the rain fly on. I guess if I wasn't worried about rain I could have left the rainfly off-
Roomy for 2 people with COTS but sleeping bag users might bet 6 in it- would be very tight.

data.title: Coleman Camp Axe
data.description: Get the right tool for multiple jobs when you're at the campsite with the Coleman Camp Axe. The sharp edge of the durable drop-forged steel head easily splits wood for a campfire or stove, while the flat edge works wonders for driving tent stakes into hard ground. When it's time to pack up, pulling your tent stakes is easy with the notch cut into the head. Durable and well built, the Coleman Camp Axe is perfect for the campsite, wooded terrain, or the backyard. The axe is equipped with a drop-forged carbon-steel axe head that easily splits wood into kindling, along with a forged steel handle and an ergonomic nonslip grip. With the Coleman Camp Axe, you'll be ready for any camping contingency.
data.reviewText:
As far as splitting logs, this thing just doesn't seem to cut it (no pun intended).
As the back side being a hammer and having a nail remover notch however, I really do like it, and it has proved more useful than the axe itself.
If you're trying to split logs with this, I would just hit the log as hard as you can and wedge the blade into the log. Then hit the back of the axe with something else to get it to split. Also definitely pick up a sharpening tool, as axes are sharpened to a different degree than knives.
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman Camp Axe

DESCRIPTION:
Get the right tool for multiple jobs when you're at the campsite with the Coleman Camp Axe. The sharp edge of the durable drop-forged steel head easily splits wood for a campfire or stove, while the flat edge works wonders for driving tent stakes into hard ground. When it's time to pack up, pulling your tent stakes is easy with the notch cut into the head. Durable and well built, the Coleman Camp Axe is perfect for the campsite, wooded terrain, or the backyard. The axe is equipped with a drop-forged carbon-steel axe head that easily splits wood into kindling, along with a forged steel handle and an ergonomic nonslip grip. REVIEW:
As far as splitting logs, this thing just doesn't seem to cut it (no pun intended).
As the back side being a hammer and having a nail remover notch however, I really do like it, and it has proved more useful than the axe itself.
If you're trying to split logs with this, I would just hit the log as hard as you can and wedge the blade into the log. Then hit the back of the axe with something else to get it to split. Also definitely pick up a sharpening tool, as axes are sharpened to a different degree than knives.

data.title: Coleman Propane Grill | RoadTrip LXE Portable Gas Grill
data.description: Stop manhandling those heavy, bulky backyard beasts and show off as the grill master with the Coleman RoadTrip LXE Propane Grill. It's easy to tote around and set up. Just fold it up, roll it with one hand to your favorite cooking spot and set it up in a matter of seconds with the lift and lock system. The grill comes equipped with durable cast-iron grilling surfaces. (Coleman RoadTrip pot support and griddles are available separately.) When you're ready to grill, push a button to ignite the InstaStart matchless lighting system. Cook for more than an hour on one propane cylinder with 20,000 BTUs of power on 285 sq. in. (1838.7 sq. cm) of cooking surface. It's perfect for BBQs, picnics, tailgating, camping or a night on the patio. When you're ready to leave, it folds down small enough to fit in the back of most car trunks.

data.title: Coleman Party Propane Grill
data.description: Enjoy easy outdoor grilling during camping trips, picnics, and tailgating parties with the Coleman Party Propane Grill. This BBQ grill combines a compact, portable design with convenient features that facilitate grilling in tough conditions. The grill's adjustable burner offers 8,000 BTUs of heat, giving you enough cooking power to prepare a variety of foods, from grilled veggies to hot dogs. The InstaStart ignition allows you to light the burner with the push of a button. Ideal for summer cookouts, this portable propane grill also excels in chilly conditions thanks to PerfectFlow technology, which helps ensure steady heat output. When you're finished grilling, the legs detach and fit inside the grill for convenient transport and storage. And thanks to its dishwasher-safe components, this camping grill is easy to clean.
data.reviewText: This is the third of these Coleman Party grills which I have purchased over the years. I have put them through their paces over the years and they are absolutely my go-to unit for simple camping (i.e. one/two people with simple meal plan). I purchased this unit without looking at all the product details. I received unit today and went looking for the pot support and nylon storage bag...both incredibly good and useful for the full enjoyment of this stove. In going back to the Amazon listing I realized that Coleman no longer ships this unit with a bag or pot support. A bit miffed in this learning, but, I wanted the complete stove...so, I thought that Coleman would be offering these items as accessories for additional cost. What I learned was that you can buy a bag, but frankly you can buy the random nylon storage sack from numerous vendors at a lower cost. The real issue is that Coleman has apparently discontinued sale of the pot support. After spending quite a bit of non-rewarding time searching the internet, I could not come up with an OEM or Non-OEM direct replacement. Very disappointing that Coleman eliminated a vitally important element from what was a GREAT stove.

data.title: Coleman 4D Rugged Personal Size Rugged Lantern
data.description:
Built for outdoor use, so it's perfect for camping, hunting, and other activity, the Coleman 4D Rugged Personal Size Lantern sheds 140 lumens of light on the High setting. Sporting a 7-watt fluorescent twin U-tube for a brighter, whiter light, the lantern runs cool to the touch for safety. It operates on 4 D batteries (not included), running for up to 23 hours on Low and 14 hours on High. Bulb replacement is a snap, thanks to the quick-release system. The Lantern's rugged design is weather-resistant and shatter-resistant, including a non-slip, rubberized handle for comfortable carrying, and heavy-duty thermoplastic and rubber construction so it stands up to heavy use in the outdoors. Lantern measures approximately 11 x 7 x 7 and weighs 1.81 pounds without batteries. The Coleman 5326A700 Rugged 4D Battery-powered U-tube Lantern lets you bring light with you wherever you go without the smell and danger of spilled fuel. The Rugged lantern runs on four D-cell batteries (not included) and provides light for up to 23 hours on low, or 14 hours on high. The seven-watt fluorescent U-tube creates a brighter and whiter light with less power. The rugged exterior is designed to be both weather-resistant and shatter-resistant and includes a non-slip rubberized grip handle, while the bulb on this Coleman lantern utilizes an unique, quick-release system that makes it easy to replace. Convenient, durable, and affordable, this battery-powered lantern is perfect for any use, inside or out. Manufacturer Warranty
Five-year warranty
data.reviewText: i would hardly call this a "personal size". It's more of a family size. used this for bike camping, way too big. Would prefer something much smaller. this would be better for car camping.

data.title: Coleman 2000026606 Insta-Clip Tube Lantern Mantle
data.description: Insta-Clip Tube Lantern Mantle, Fits Lantern Models #2000 Series & Model #2500.
data.reviewText:
$6.60 to ship a 4 dollar mantle?

OK - positives - the single mantle and new northstar electric start lanterns are like having a lamp around and work great - light output is aboiut 100w or more on high, and they can be turned way down low for a night light.

The mantle leaves something to be desired.  First time I "clipped" mine on everything was fine - ready to do the burnoff.  Ash was fine after the burnoff.  Time to light up.  Light went fine until the tie clips got red hot and thats when problems started.  There's something called the coefficient of thermal expansion.  Take the heat into consideration and pow, the clip undoes itself, wrecknig the mantle at the same time.

Ok - now its time to buy a new mantle.  4 buck mantle - 6.60 to ship it to me.  10.60 for a piece of ash.  I think its cheaper in nevada.

My parting advice - make sure you really tie those clips on good and tight and snug before you burn the mantle the first time.  Next time I find a mantle somewhere (of course nobody stocks these in the store) I'm going to take a pair of pliers and twist those little spring things so they can stay put in the groove provided in the lamp.

Maybe then my price/hr of usage will go down.  It costs more for mantles now than for propane!
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman 2000026606 Insta-Clip Tube Lantern Mantle

DESCRIPTION:
Insta-Clip Tube Lantern Mantle, Fits Lantern Models #2000 Series & Model #2500.

REVIEW:
$6.60 to ship a 4 dollar mantle?

OK - positives - the single mantle and new northstar electric start lanterns are like having a lamp around and work great - light output is aboiut 100w or more on high, and they can be turned way down low for a night light.

The mantle leaves something to be desired.  First time I "clipped" mine on everything was fine - ready to do the burnoff.  Ash was fine after the burnoff.  Time to light up.  Light went fine until the tie clips got red hot and thats when problems started.  There's something called the coefficient of thermal expansion.  Take the heat into consideration and pow, the clip undoes itself, wrecknig the mantle at the same time.

Ok - now its time to buy a new mantle.  4 buck mantle - 6.60 to ship it to me.  10.60 for a piece of ash.  I think its cheaper in nevada.

My parting advice - make sure you really tie those clips on good and tight and snug before you burn the mantle the first time.  Next time I find a mantle somewhere (of course nobody stocks these in the store) I'm going to take a pair of pliers and twist those little spring things so they can stay put in the groove provided in the lamp.

Maybe then my price/hr of usage will go down.  It costs more for mantles now than for propane!

data.reviewText: I ordered two of these so my wife and I can sleep on the porch now that it is cooler. They seem to be very well made and are comfortable. Time will tell. The only thing about them is that they are not really long beds. I am 6'2" and I cannot stretch out. I think the tallest person that can lay on the coat straight would be 5'6". Not a real problem. data.title: Coleman Camp Oven
data.description:
Bring the joy of fresh-baked muffins, cinnamon rolls, cornbread, and other treats to any campsite with the Coleman Camp Oven. Designed to fit neatly on Coleman propane and liquid fuel camp stoves, this portable oven gives you the ability to bake treats or keep food warm while spending time outdoors. The included interior rack adjusts to 3 heights, while the easy-to-read thermometer lets you bake with confidence. This portable oven is made from smooth aluminized steel that resists corrosion and scratches and wipes clean easily, and it folds flat for convenient carrying and storage when its time to pack up camp. Camping stoves and other cooking accessories have made it possible to cook most things outdoors, but baked goods have always been out of the question. Until now, that is. Made of smooth aluminized steel, the Coleman camping oven lets you bake a batch of muffins, biscuits, or rolls right at the campsite.


The Coleman camping oven is designed to sit on a two- or three-burner camping stove.
The oven doesn't require electric or gas power to work. Instead, campers simply place the oven on top of a Coleman two- or three-burner stove, which provides all the heat you need to bake your favorite dishes. The oven also offers such baking essentials as an adjustable steel bake rack and an easy-to-read thermometer. And when you're done baking, the oven folds flat for easy storage. Made in China, the Coleman camping oven measures 12 by 12 by 12 inches (W x H x D) when standing and weighs 7 pounds. About Coleman
More than 100 years ago, a young man with an entrepreneurial spirit and a better idea began manufacturing lanterns in Wichita, Kansas. His name was W.C. Coleman, and the company he founded would change life in America. A man plagued with such poor vision he sometimes had to ask classmates to read aloud to him, Coleman saw a brilliant light in 1900 in a drugstore window that stopped him in his tracks. He inquired about the light inside and discovered he was able to read even the small print on a medicine bottle by the illumination. The lamps had mantles, not wicks, and were fueled by gasoline under pressure instead of coal oil. Soon afterward, Coleman started a lighting service that offered a "no light, no pay" clause--a big step forward for merchants who were burned by inferior products that rarely worked--and drew substantial interest from businesses that wanted to keep their lights on after dark. In the ensuing years, Coleman expanded its product line well beyond lanterns. The company's current catalog is thick with products that make spending time outdoors a pleasure. There are coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days on end, comfortable airbeds that won't deflate during the night, a complete line of LED lights that last for years, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and much, much more. Coleman has truly fashioned much of our outdoor camping experience, and expects to do so for generations to come.
data.reviewText: The temp gage shows approximately 50 degrees cooler than the actual temp. Made with very thin material. It works with some practice and after determining the actual temp.

data.title: Coleman EasyStay Single High Airbed
data.description: Pack lightweight support with a Coleman Quickbed Lite Airbed. With its internal coils, you ll rest on superior comfort and support, while a soft top offers relaxation for sleepers who are on the go. Once you determine your perfect firmness, it will be yours all night long with the airtight system that's guaranteed not to leak and the easy to use double lock valve that also helps inflate and deflate the bed quickly. When you're ready to pack up, the built in storage system makes putting the bed away a matter of folding it, rolling it and storing it.
data.reviewText: Sleep comfortably for 5 nights during our family camping but need to inflate after couple of nights to adjust the firmness of the airbed. Not bad after all, exactly what the product info stated.

data.title: Coleman Dome Tent for Camping | Sundome Tent with Easy Setup
data.description: " The Coleman Sundome 4-Person Tent is designed for quick and easy setup, so you can spend more time enjoying the outdoors. Great for camping in warm weather, this backpacking tent is designed with large windows and a ground vent to help push warm air up and out to keep you comfortable. The WeatherTec system with patented welded floors and inverted seams helps ensure you stay dry if the weather turns, while the sturdy frame with Insta-Clip pole attachments stands up to 35+ mph winds. A rainfly with an integrated door awning is included to help keep rain from getting into the tent, and the durable Polyguard 2X double-thick fabric is designed to withstand the elements for reliable use season after season. This dome tent offers enough room for one queen size air mattress and comes with built-in storage pockets to help keep your gear organized. An E-Port makes it easy to bring electrical power inside the tent. The Coleman Sundome 4-Person Tent sets up in only 10 minutes thanks to snag-free continuous pole sleeves and packs away compactly in the included carry bag.
data.reviewText: While the tent was cheap and affordable, it was very easily pushed around by a moderate amount of wind. Due to this, the tent was almost flattened on one side. Attaching the guyline did not do much to remedy this situation. I would not recommend this tent to others.
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman Dome Tent for Camping | Sundome Tent with Easy Setup

DESCRIPTION:
" The Coleman Sundome 4-Person Tent is designed for quick and easy setup, so you can spend more time enjoying the outdoors. Great for camping in warm weather, this backpacking tent is designed with large windows and a ground vent to help push warm air up and out to keep you comfortable. The WeatherTec system with patented welded floors and inverted seams helps ensure you stay dry if the weather turns, while the sturdy frame with Insta-Clip pole attachments stands up to 35+ mph winds. A rainfly with an integrated door awning is included to help keep rain from getting into the tent, and the durable Polyguard 2X double-thick fabric is designed to withstand the elements for reliable use season after season. This dome tent offers enough room for one queen size air mattress and comes with built-in storage pockets to help keep your gear organized. An E-Port makes it easy to bring electrical power inside the tent. The Coleman Sundome 4-Person Tent sets up in only 10 minutes thanks to snag-free continuous pole sleeves and packs away compactly in the included carry bag.

REVIEW:
While the tent was cheap and affordable, it was very easily pushed around by a moderate amount of wind. Due to this, the tent was almost flattened on one side. Attaching the guyline did not do much to remedy this situation. I would not recommend this tent to others.

data.title: Coleman 16-Can Soft Cooler with Removable Liner
data.description: The Coleman 16-Can Soft Cooler goes with you to picnics, BBQs, camping, sporting events, tailgates and beyond. It holds 16 cans inside, and features plenty of extra storage space in its front zippered pocket, side mesh pockets, and mesh pocket in the lid. There are even bungees on the lid for stowing more extras! Sporting an adjustable shoulder strap, the cooler is soft on the outside for comfortable carrying, and has a hard plastic liner inside to protect your food from being crushed. The liner material is FDA approved for food contact, and is removable for easy cleaning. The Soft Cooler provides antimicrobial protection, to resist odors, mold, and mildew.
data.reviewText: It's skinnier than I anticipated and taller. I was hoping it to be wider and shorter but it works very well.

data.title: Coleman 2 Burner Dual Fuel Compact Liquid Fuel Stove
data.description: Stove, 2-Burner Standard Compact, Dual Fuel. Whether you're staying in a cabin, tent, or under the stars, the Coleman 424-700G Two-burner Compact Dual-fuel Stove will enhance any camping adventure. Equipped with one 7,500 BTU burner and one 6,500 BTU burner, you can whip up a meal in minutes. The Coleman stove operates on clean-burning Coleman Fuel or unleaded gas and one tank gives you two hours of burn time. This stove can boil a quart of water in four minutes. All this in an amazingly small package of 17.75-inches by 11.5-inches by 4.875-inches when closed for easy packing.
data.reviewText: Excellent stove.  Especially for making larger more complicated meals.
