data.title: Coleman 4D Rugged Personal Size Rugged Lantern
data.description:
Built for outdoor use, so it's perfect for camping, hunting, and other activity, the Coleman 4D Rugged Personal Size Lantern sheds 140 lumens of light on the High setting. Sporting a 7-watt fluorescent twin U-tube for a brighter, whiter light, the lantern runs cool to the touch for safety. It operates on 4 D batteries (not included), running for up to 23 hours on Low and 14 hours on High. Bulb replacement is a snap, thanks to the quick-release system. The Lantern's rugged design is weather-resistant and shatter-resistant, including a non-slip, rubberized handle for comfortable carrying, and heavy-duty thermoplastic and rubber construction so it stands up to heavy use in the outdoors. Lantern measures approximately 11 x 7 x 7 and weighs 1.81 pounds without batteries. The Coleman 5326A700 Rugged 4D Battery-powered U-tube Lantern lets you bring light with you wherever you go without the smell and danger of spilled fuel. The Rugged lantern runs on four D-cell batteries (not included) and provides light for up to 23 hours on low, or 14 hours on high. The seven-watt fluorescent U-tube creates a brighter and whiter light with less power. The rugged exterior is designed to be both weather-resistant and shatter-resistant and includes a non-slip rubberized grip handle, while the bulb on this Coleman lantern utilizes an unique, quick-release system that makes it easy to replace. Convenient, durable, and affordable, this battery-powered lantern is perfect for any use, inside or out. Manufacturer Warranty
Five-year warranty
data.reviewText: Given as a gift

data.title: Coleman Propane Grill | RoadTrip LXE Portable Gas Grill
data.description: Stop manhandling those heavy, bulky backyard beasts and show off as the grill master with the Coleman RoadTrip LXE Propane Grill. It's easy to tote around and set up. Just fold it up, roll it with one hand to your favorite cooking spot and set it up in a matter of seconds with the lift and lock system. The grill comes equipped with durable cast-iron grilling surfaces. (Coleman RoadTrip pot support and griddles are available separately.) When you're ready to grill, push a button to ignite the InstaStart matchless lighting system. Cook for more than an hour on one propane cylinder with 20,000 BTUs of power on 285 sq. in. (1838.7 sq. cm) of cooking surface. It's perfect for BBQs, picnics, tailgating, camping or a night on the patio. When you're ready to leave, it folds down small enough to fit in the back of most car trunks.
data.reviewText: Great concept, nice looking and once you learn how to use it, cooks ok.  Problem lies in the fact that it is a real pain to clean and after about 10 uses, the chrome pan is rusting.  Oh well, probably get 3 or 4 years out of it.
data.text:

data.title: Coleman Hooligan Backpacking Tent
data.description: The Hooligan 2 from Coleman is a 2 person backpacking tent. It features a full coverage coated polyester fly (450mm) that envelops the tent in a cocoon of protection from the harshest weather. A small front vestibule offers some storage space for extra gear and the all mesh body is great for ventilation and stargazing on nights when the weather is clear. The fly seams are sealed at the factory and the rugged 1000D polyethylene floor is a bathtub style (wraps up the sides) for extreme weather protection and has welded seams, even in the corners, to guard against leaks. Set up is with a single pole and thus could not be more simple and quick. Great for beginner backpackers, scouts and those who want to keep it simple.Non Freestanding (1 pole) design is simple to set up and take down.All mesh body for maximum ventilation and comfort and reduces condensation.Zippered back vent in the fly helps to keep the tent comfortable - adjustable through zipper on the back tent wall.Sturdy 11mm (1ea) Shockcorded fiberglass frame for easy set up.All mesh body is perfect for stargazing on nice nights.Full coverage fly is perfect for harsh weather.1 Small storage vestibule - 6 sq. ft. - good for boots etc..1 interior storage pocket.Easy convenient continuous pole sleeve construction.Pin and ring pole to body connection is fast and simple.External guy points to keep the tent stable in storms and high winds.Instructions sewn to carry bag so they are easy to find and hard to lose.All carry sacks and stakes included.Includes convenient flashlight loop on the ceiling.Fire retardant.Import..
data.reviewText: Tent is bigger then I thought, I am 1.65 meters in height and there is enough space for 3-4 people too(but not for big and tall people, but for people like me)

data.title: Coleman 2000014864 Mosquito Head Net
data.description: Enjoy the outdoors without the pests when you're wearing a Coleman Mosquito Head Net. The fine mesh stops insects from biting, while still allowing you to see nature's beauty. The Mosquito Head Net is designed especially for wide, all-around brim hats, but still fits other styles well. Keep flying insects out when hiking or working in mosquito- or gnat-ridden territory with the Coleman insect head net. The net is designed to fit comfortably over most headgear, and is made with a 1mm maximum netting grade.
data.reviewText:
The top part where it sits on the crown of your head is camoflauge patterned. Sort of a dark multicam pattern.

The mesh material itself isn't very fine. It's not "No-see-um" proof, so the smaller insects can get inside. The holes are just too big.

I would presume a juvenile mosquito could, if it tried, barely squeeze in there, but it should hold out the ticks.

data.title: Coleman Tent Kit
data.description: Set up the tent quickly with the steel tent pegs and rubber mallet. Keep it clean for the entire campout with the nesting hand broom and dustpan. When the fun is done, use the tent stake puller to get the pegs out of the ground easily. All four of these important tools fit nicely inside the mesh carry bag for the next adventure.
data.reviewText:
This kit seems like a decent idea. While some of it is, the stakes (specifically the tops) just don't hold up.

The spike part of the stakes is fine. However if you're just a hair off the cheap plastic tops will snap off in a heartbeat. Whether staking down or pulling out.

To be honest, the generic, full plastic stakes that came with the tent held up better. They may not last real long, but for how quick these can break you can buy a couple buckets of the general plastic ones.

Naturally if you are camping in more rugged environments, you'll be spending the extra money for the really good stakes. However for the general use car camper, I'd look past this set and load up on the cheaper, heavier plastic ones. The lack of quality in the hook/eye portion of the stakes with this kit just aren't worth the price.
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman Tent Kit

DESCRIPTION:
Set up the tent quickly with the steel tent pegs and rubber mallet. Keep it clean for the entire campout with the nesting hand broom and dustpan. When the fun is done, use the tent stake puller to get the pegs out of the ground easily. All four of these important tools fit nicely inside the mesh carry bag for the next adventure.

REVIEW:
This kit seems like a decent idea. While some of it is, the stakes (specifically the tops) just don't hold up.

The spike part of the stakes is fine. However if you're just a hair off the cheap plastic tops will snap off in a heartbeat. Whether staking down or pulling out.

To be honest, the generic, full plastic stakes that came with the tent held up better. They may not last real long, but for how quick these can break you can buy a couple buckets of the general plastic ones.

Naturally if you are camping in more rugged environments, you'll be spending the extra money for the really good stakes. However for the general use car camper, I'd look past this set and load up on the cheaper, heavier plastic ones. The lack of quality in the hook/eye portion of the stakes with this kit just aren't worth the price.

data.title: Coleman 8-Person Tent for Camping | Red Canyon Car Camping Tent
data.description: Measuring 17- by 10-feet, Coleman's Red Canyon tent is spacious enough to comfortably sleep eight campers. Ideal for weekend camping trips with family and friends, the Red Canyon features removable privacy walls, allowing you to create up to three separate rooms.
data.reviewText: For about $100 dollars great value. We are a family of four with two small children. We bought this tent to have enough space in the event of a rain day. I was disappointed after the first rain, of pooling along the inside edges of the tent. I was disappointed that directions need to be better. The tent does indeed pack back with all accessories into the carry bag included. I do not camp often, so I wanted a tent that I did not have to waterproof. Because of the claim by Coleman with their Weathertec water proofing, I was more confident in my purchase. I did see the rain bead and run off the tent fabric. But after awhile, the fabric below the rain fly and above the floor tub became saturated and wet to the touch inside. Also, on the very cool nights, this same section became wet to the touch inside the tent. The fabric under the rain fly never was wet and never had condensation in the morning. The ventilation is okay if there is a good breeze, otherwise the sun really heats up the  tent. Since the rain fly is velcroed to tents poles, guywired to ground stakes and attached to tent pole rings with bungee cord, removing the rain fly was not practical. It is really better to be outside the tent to remain cool than to rely on the mesh to keep the tent cool. This was our experience. We had battery operated fans which helped. I returned the tent after one week of use and two rainy nights primarily due to the water issues.

The ventilation is okay if there is a good breeze, otherwise the sun really heats up the  tent. Since the rain fly is velcroed to tents poles, guywired to ground stakes and attached to tent pole rings with bungee cord, removing the rain fly was not practical. It is really better to be outside the tent to remain cool than to rely on the mesh to keep the tent cool. This was our experience. We had battery operated fans which helped. data.title: Coleman StormBeam Dynamo Lantern with Radio and Cell Phone Chargers
data.description: Strong, reliable, and easy to use, Coleman's StormBeam Lanterns are designed specifically for use during power outages, storms, and other emergencies. The StormBeam Dynamo Lantern With Radio and Cell Phone Chargers has a built-in crank to recharge the lantern with no outside power source, so you'll always have light when you need it. Just one minute of hand-cranking yields 15 minutes of high-powered light. This StormBeam Lantern features a built-in FM radio with scan function, to help you track weather reports and developing emergency situations. What's more, the Lantern comes with adapters for the 6 most common types of cell-phone chargers (stored inside lantern cap), so you can use the Lantern crank to charge your phone -- vital in an emergency. The Dynamo's bright 0.7-watt LED sheds strong, reliable light and will never need to be replaced. The lantern's battery pack (included) offers consistent charging. The StormBeam Lantern's convenient molded-grip carrying handle folds under the lantern when not in use, for compact storage.
data.reviewText: Mine came defective and drove me nuts for awhile cause I would follow the instructions exactly and the light would stay on for like 1 min or 2 then start dimming to a worthless glow. Finally I just upped and bought one at Wal-mart the same exact thing and it worked fine. So as with all electrical stuff beware ordering them on internet as they can come beat up and may be a hassle to return. But Amazon has good customer service so your covered chief. Thank You Amazon .. never have so many owed so much to so few....

data.title: Coleman RoadTrip Swaptop Aluminum Grill Griddle, Full Size
data.description: Get more meals out of your Coleman RoadTrip Grill by adding a durable Coleman RoadTrip Swaptop Full Size Aluminum Griddle. Swaptop compatible, you can mix and match it with other cook tops (sold separately) for more meal options. It creates 285 sq. in. of cooking space for pancakes, bacon and more.
data.reviewText:
This is almost a guaranteed MUST HAVE for any family camping trip. I was able to make pancakes in the mornings with the kids, sausage links, among many other things. I also planned on making eggs with it, but the griddle wasn't deep enough, which wasn't a problem as I had bought the Stove Grate as well.

Typically the middle area doesn't get nearly as hot as it does around the two burner spots, so things in the middle will take longer to cook? Maybe use that for the adults as by the time the kids food will be finished cooking, the middle should be as well.

Overall a good piece and makes terrific pancakes and grilled cheese!

Typically the middle area doesn't get nearly as hot as it does around the two burner spots, so things in the middle will take longer to cook? Maybe use that for the adults as by the time the kids food will be finished cooking, the middle should be as well.

data.reviewText: Fits the stove fine as pictured, but they leave off the fuel canister!  It cannot just hang in midair! Also, the stove legs don't really fit snug into anything, they just rest there, and it seems as if with the rounded edges of my stove the slightest bump will cause it to slip through at any time.  I will not be using it unless I find a piece of wood to make it into a table top that would hold the canister and keep the stove from slipping through.  It is a bit wobbly.

data.title: Coleman Deluxe PerfectFlow Propane Lantern
data.description:
Illuminate your outdoor adventures with the Coleman Deluxe PerfectFlow Propane Lantern. This powerful camping lantern produces 967 lumens of ultra-bright light. You can light the lantern with a quick match strike and dial in the desired brightness level with the adjustable control knob. The footed base keeps the lantern stable when placed on a tabletop or other surface, and the feet on the base collapse for compact storage. Thanks to PerfectFlow pressure-control technology, this propane lantern delivers a steady fuel stream and consistent performance in all kinds of weather. This outdoor lantern uses a 16.4-ounce propane cylinder (sold separately) and lasts 7 hours on the high setting or 13 hours on low. Coleman's Two-Mantle Propane Lantern is as steady as they come. This lantern is pressure-regulated to control fuel flow, so you get consistent light in any temperature. This Coleman propane lantern lights with a match and has a control knob that works like a dimmer switch to let you adjust the brightness and burn time. The porcelain ventilators will prevent rusting and help this lantern last you a long time. This lantern operates on a 16.4-ounce cylinder of Coleman propane (not included), and will burn for up to seven hours on high or 14 hours on low. With a unique, stable base and foldable feet it just doesn't get much more convenient than this. Manufacturer's Warranty
Limited lifetime warranty About Coleman
The Coleman Company has been creating and innovating products for recreational outdoor use since W.C. Coleman started selling gasoline-powered lanterns in 1900. Inventor of the hugely popular fold-up camp stove, Coleman developed a plastic liner for his galvanized steel coolers in 1957--the birth of the modern cooler--and the company has been improving their utility and design ever since. The array of products that bear the Coleman name now includes just about everything you might need to work or play outdoors, from tents and sleeping bags to boats, backpacks, and furniture.
data.reviewText: The lantern I received is a bit crooked when screwed on to a gas tank. And the stand cup it sits in isan't wide enough . Not the same stand cup in the picture. I don't even care though because it certainly works good and its bright enough with out the need to say I need more light!

data.title: Coleman 70-Quart Xtreme Cooler
data.description: When you need a tough cooler with extraordinary ice retention, choose a Coleman 70-quart Xtreme Cooler. Extra insulation retains ice for five days in temperatures up to 90 degrees Fahrenheit and still performs incredibly well on even hotter days. The insulating foam is free from CFCs, HFCs and HCFCs, which deplete the ozone. Inside, there's plenty of room for all your food and drinks, including 100 cans. A hinged EZ-Clean lid with four cup holders on top keeps your drinks close, with two-way handles make carrying easier. The Xtreme cooler doubles as a bench to provide extra seating.Antimicrobial additive protects liner from odor-causing bacteriaEZ-Clean lid has four cup holdersSturdy lid doubles as an extra seatRust-proof and leak-resistantHolds 2 liter bottles uprightBrand: ColemanSet includes: One (1) coolerColor: Blue/white, Grey/orangeMaterials: Plastic, foamDimensions: 17.7 inches high x 28.1 inches wide x 15.5 inches deepModel: 3000002012
data.reviewText:
Pros:
It's sturdy.
It kept food cold on a 3 day camping trip in 50-70 degree weather.
It's not an awful, gaudy color.

Cons:
It's really heavy when full of food and ice, and I wish I'd purchased one with integrated wheels.

With the Coleman Camp Axe, you'll be ready for any camping contingency.

data.title: Coleman Divide Plus Spin 525L LED Lantern
data.description: The Coleman Divide Plus Spin 525L LED Lantern features a unique twisting design that lets you rotate the entire lantern to easily change modes.Impact-resistant up to 1 m.
data.reviewText: This light does have some nice features. It is bright--525 lumens at the brightest setting and can be dimmed simply by twisting the top. It can be hung as an overhead light using the fold out hook in the base. The batteries can be completely disengaged by a twist of the base, thus maintaining battery life between use. Now the downsides: this is made of lightweight plastic and I never would have figured out how to open and close the battery compartment without instructions. This does not bode well for the future when I want to change the battery and the instructions are long gone. Moreover, though my expectations may have been unrealistic with this item, they were not quite met. First if I'd looked more carefully I would have realized all the info didn't match up: currently in the product specifications, this is listed as requiring one lithium ion battery which was included. In fact, as stated elsewhere, this requires 4 D batteries which are not included. Also, I already have a Streamlight 44931 The Siege Lantern from Amazon which I Iove! I was hoping this would be of similar quality, though this Coleman product is less expensive. In fact it is made of lightweight plastic and feels not very durable in comparison to the substantial, easy-grip feel of the Seige Lantern. In all fairness, the Coleman is brighter and slightly less expensive, but I have a strong preference for the Streamlight product and suggest you look it up and compare. The difference is well worth the additional expense, in my opinion, if you can get by without the extra brightness.

data.title: Coleman Propane Grill | RoadTrip LXE Portable Gas Grill
data.description: Stop manhandling those heavy, bulky backyard beasts and show off as the grill master with the Coleman RoadTrip LXE Propane Grill. It's easy to tote around and set up. Just fold it up, roll it with one hand to your favorite cooking spot and set it up in a matter of seconds with the lift and lock system. The grill comes equipped with durable cast-iron grilling surfaces. (Coleman RoadTrip pot support and griddles are available separately.) When you're ready to grill, push a button to ignite the InstaStart matchless lighting system. Cook for more than an hour on one propane cylinder with 20,000 BTUs of power on 285 sq. in. (1838.7 sq. cm) of cooking surface. It's perfect for BBQs, picnics, tailgating, camping or a night on the patio. When you're ready to leave, it folds down small enough to fit in the back of most car trunks.
data.reviewText: I got this grill as a gift from my wife for tailgating and it works great.  My friend also got one and we cooked for 20-30 people at our tailgates this season for six games.  No problems cooking a variety of food and I didn't experience any trouble with hot spots or putting the lid down.  I have a couple minor complaints.  One is that the hooks on the front of the grill are so wide that they are pretty useless for hanging cooking utensils on.  They just won't fit.  I had to set them on the side slide out side shelves, which are awesome, so they take up space I could be using for something else.  Also the latch that locks the cover never fails to open when I'm wheeling the grill around.  That is super annoying because the grates fall out then you get your hands dirty picking them up.  With that said I can't recommend this grill enough.  It was perfect for tailgating and never let me down.

data.title: Coleman Carlsbad Tent with Screen Room
data.description: Sleep in after the sun rises or put the kids to bed early while the sun is still up with a Coleman Carlsbad Fast Pitch Dome Tent with Screen Room. The Dark Room technology on this tent blocks 90% of sunlight to keep the tent darker if you want to sleep while the sun is up, and it also reduces how much the tent heats up during the day by up to 11.4%.
data.reviewText:
this is a good tent for people who want to bring some of the conveniences of home to a campsite. The tent itself is relatively easy to pitch. It is descried as a four person tent, which may be a tight fit for four average or larger adults. Two to three adults could fit comfortably or may  an adult and a couple of little kids. It has some nice features including an eport which allows access to power. The major selling point is the dark room feature. And it is dark. As a sper light sleeper I wish I had that feature in my bedroom but in all honesty one of the charms of camping is waking to the light of the early morning sun,. Still it probably is effective at helping kids sleep and certainly will block any light. The tent also has a screened in porch. It is not waterproof or weather resistant but is an enjoyable feature.

This is a good tent for people who enjoy camping but not necessarily roughing it. It is too heavy for backpacking but great for campsite use. It is roomy and  designed for comfort. It is well made and should provide years of enjoyment. .

data.title: Coleman 9-Can Soft Cooler with Removable Liner
data.description: Keep food and drinks cold with the Coleman Soft Cooler. This compact, portable cooler bag holds up to nine cans and offers plenty of space for a family picnic or game-day snacks. Heat-welded seams in the zippered main compartment prevent leaks and maintain a cold environment, while the removable hard plastic liner fits snugly inside the soft cooler and allows you to easily dump out any ice melt. When not using the hard plastic liner, you can use ice substitutes to keep contents cold and dry. The cooler bag's easy-clean interior has an antimicrobial lining to resist formation of mold, mildew, fungus, and odor. A zippered front pocket, pocket under the lid, and two side mesh pockets make it easy to carry water bottles, snacks, silverware, and other accessories. You can also secure items to the top with the convenient bungee cords built into the lid. An adjustable shoulder strap lets you comfortably carry the cooler anywhere you go.
data.reviewText: Purchased for hubby's lunch tote, to replace his worn out one. It's smaller than his original tote. Too costly to return, so we will keep it for traveling in our car or trips outside for a picnic. Only large enough to carry a couple of bottles of water and a few snacks.

data.title: Coleman Self-Inflating Camp Pad with Pillow
data.description:
Sleep on a layer of air while camping, with the Coleman Self-Inflating Camp Pad With Pillow. Measuring 25" wide by 76" long by 2.5" high when inflated, the Camp Pad features a convenient built-in pillow and tufted design for added comfort. The Camp Pad's free-flow valve makes for easy inflation and deflation. It's constructed with a sturdy nylon shell for durability and weather-resistance. The attached compression straps make for easy transport and storage. Perfect for camping, weekends at the cabin, or even at home to accommodate an overflow of unexpected guests, Coleman's self-inflating pad inflates with the twist of a valve to create a comfortable, 2-inch thick sleeping pad. A pillow offers additional comfort while the 70D nylon, spot-bonded surface will keep you comfortable throughout the night. Measuring 76 inches long by 24.5 inches wide, this five-pound mat rolls up for convenient storage and offers compression straps. About Coleman
The Coleman Company has been creating and innovating products for recreational outdoor use since W.C. Coleman started selling gasoline-powered lanterns in 1900. Inventor of the hugely popular fold-up camp stove, Coleman developed a plastic liner for his galvanized steel coolers in 1957--the birth of the modern cooler--and the company has been improving their utility and design ever since. The array of products that bear the Coleman name now includes just about everything you might need to work or play outdoors, from tents and sleeping bags to boats, backpacks, and furniture.
data.reviewText:
I bought this as an afterthought -- as in, "Oh yeah, I need one of those too!  Almost forgot".

Since it was a fast purchase, I didn't research, figuring Coleman to be good regardless.  WRONG!

1.  Not self-inflating as I think of self-inflating.  There is a thin foam pad that compresses when the mat is rolled up, and it expands again, letting in a bit of air, when the valve is open.

2.  It is ALSO self-DEflating!  I learned this while camping.  There are no holes in it; I figure the valve must be faulty.  I woke up with only that thin foam insert between me and the ground.  My body weight forced the air out.

3.  The pillow is good.  I like the pillow.

I would not recommend this to even an enemy.  I'm going to a closed-cell foam pad, and if I can find the receipt, it's definitely going back.

I'm very unhappy with Coleman.

Josh
data.text:
TITLE: Coleman Self-Inflating Camp Pad with Pillow DESCRIPTION: Sleep on a layer of air while camping, with the Coleman Self-Inflating Camp Pad With Pillow. Measuring 25" wide by 76" long by 2.5" high when inflated, the Camp Pad features a convenient built-in pillow and tufted design for added comfort. The Camp Pad's free-flow valve makes for easy inflation and deflation. It's constructed with a sturdy nylon shell for durability and weather-resistance. The attached compression straps make for easy transport and storage. Perfect for camping, weekends at the cabin, or even at home to accommodate an overflow of unexpected guests, Coleman's self-inflating pad inflates with the twist of a valve to create a comfortable, 2-inch thick sleeping pad. A pillow offers additional comfort while the 70D nylon, spot-bonded surface will keep you comfortable throughout the night. Measuring 76 inches long by 24.5 inches wide, this five-pound mat rolls up for convenient storage and offers compression straps. About Coleman
The Coleman Company has been creating and innovating products for recreational outdoor use since W.C. Coleman started selling gasoline-powered lanterns in 1900. Inventor of the hugely popular fold-up camp stove, Coleman developed a plastic liner for his galvanized steel coolers in 1957--the birth of the modern cooler--and the company has been improving their utility and design ever since. The array of products that bear the Coleman name now includes just about everything you might need to work or play outdoors, from tents and sleeping bags to boats, backpacks, and furniture. REVIEW: I bought this as an afterthought -- as in, "Oh yeah, I need one of those too! Almost forgot". Since it was a fast purchase, I didn't research, figuring Coleman to be good regardless. WRONG! 1. Not self-inflating as I think of self-inflating. There is a thin foam pad that compresses when the mat is rolled up, and it expands again, letting in a bit of air, when the valve is open. 2. It is ALSO self-DEflating! I learned this while camping. There are no holes in it; I figure the valve must be faulty. I woke up with only that thin foam insert between me and the ground. My body weight forced the air out. 3. The pillow is good. I like the pillow. I would not recommend this to even an enemy. I'm going to a closed-cell foam pad, and if I can find the receipt, it's definitely going back. I'm very unhappy with Coleman. Josh

data.title: Coleman Camp Axe
data.description: Get the right tool for multiple jobs when you're at the campsite with the Coleman Camp Axe. The sharp edge of the durable drop-forged steel head easily splits wood for a campfire or stove, while the flat edge works wonders for driving tent stakes into hard ground. When it's time to pack up, pulling your tent stakes is easy with the notch cut into the head. Durable and well built, the Coleman Camp Axe is perfect for the campsite, wooded terrain, or the backyard. The axe is equipped with a drop-forged carbon-steel axe head that easily splits wood into kindling, along with a forged steel handle and an ergonomic nonslip grip. With the Coleman Camp Axe, you'll be ready for any camping contingency.

data.title: Coleman Quad LED Lantern Special Edition Ultra Bright 190 Lumens, Red
data.description:
Take the convenience of four lights in one with you with the Coleman Quad LED Lantern. Together the lantern shines 190 ultra-bright lumens for up to 75 hours. During an emergency, pop one of the four lights off the base for a personal flashlight. They recharge once theyre back on the base for hands-free light. The 24 LEDs last a lifetime, so they never need replacing and the lantern runs cool so its always safe to touch, even when its been on for hours. Combining an area light with four portable lanterns in a single clever unit, the Coleman LED Quad lantern is a versatile choice for indoor or outdoor use. The Quad is equipped with four removable LED panels that hold 24 LED lights total. Users can remove any of the four panels and use them independently, such as when needing a portable light for the bathroom or tent, or they can keep all four attached to the lantern and produce 190 total lumens of light. As a result, the Quad is the perfect light for group camping trips and any other occasion where folks might splinter off into smaller groups during the night. Each of the panels is a fully functioning light, with six 5mm white LEDs, a rechargeable NiMH battery, an independent on/off switch, and a top handle for carrying or hanging. The NiMH battery--which recharges when stored in the lantern base--provides up to 1.5 hours of light on its own. The lantern base, meanwhile, requires eight D-cell batteries (not included), which provide an approximate run time of 75 hours for the entire lantern. Other features include a main on/off switch located on top of the lantern, a light range of 26 feet, and a limited five-year warranty. About Coleman
More than 100 years ago, a young man with an entrepreneurial spirit and a better idea began manufacturing lanterns in Wichita, Kansas. His name was W.C. Coleman, and the company he founded would change life in America. A man plagued with such poor vision he sometimes had to ask classmates to read aloud to him, Coleman saw a brilliant light in 1900 in a drugstore window that stopped him in his tracks. He inquired about the light inside and discovered he was able to read even the small print on a medicine bottle by the illumination. The lamps had mantles, not wicks, and were fueled by gasoline under pressure instead of coal oil. Soon afterward, Coleman started a lighting service that offered a "no light, no pay" clause--a big step forward for merchants who were burned by inferior products that rarely worked--and drew substantial interest from businesses that wanted to keep their lights on after dark. In the ensuing years, Coleman expanded its product line well beyond lanterns. The company's current catalog is thick with products that make spending time outdoors a pleasure. There are coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days on end, comfortable airbeds that won't deflate during the night, a complete line of LED lights that last for years, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and much, much more. Coleman has truly fashioned much of our outdoor camping experience, and expects to do so for generations to come.
data.reviewText:
Pretty pricey and doesn't give off nearly as much light as a fuel lantern. Wouldn't buy again for the price. Would go with the propane lanterns all day over this if you want brightness. big and bulky and batteries burn up fast.

-g

data.title: Coleman Steel-Belted Portable Cooler, 54 Quart
data.description: Go back in time with cold food and drinks today when you take a Coleman 54 Quart Steel Belted Cooler along with you. It looks just like the one you remember with its classic design first developed in 1954. Large enough to hold 85 cans and tall enough to hold 2-liter bottles upright, you'll have plenty of space for refreshments for a whole group of people. Whether you're headed on an overnight camping trip, to a tailgate party, or out to a BBQ, the comfort-grip steel handles and stainless steel lid latch make getting to your destination easy, comfortable and secure. Once you arrive, the Have-A-Seat lid will ensure you always have a place to sit and relax while you enjoy the day with a cold drink in hand. When the fun is done, the leak-resistant channel drain makes it easy to empty any excess water without tilting the cooler, so you're ready for your next fun adventure. A Coleman Steel Belted Cooler will rekindle your fondest outdoor memories  and equip you to make many new ones.
data.reviewText: The Coleman 54 quart steel-sided cooler immediately takes you back to a different time.  It is wonderfully built, with metal handles riveted to the body of the unit.  The metal latch is a major plus, as this cooler will do most of its duty for me riding in the back of a side-by-side.  While the price may initially scare some, compare this cooler with other all-plastic coolers of similar size.  The difference isn't much compared with what you get.

TITLE:
Coleman Steel-Belted Portable Cooler, 54 Quart

DESCRIPTION:
Go back in time with cold food and drinks today when you take a Coleman 54 Quart Steel Belted Cooler along with you. It looks just like the one you remember with its classic design first developed in 1954. Large enough to hold 85 cans and tall enough to hold 2-liter bottles upright, you'll have plenty of space for refreshments for a whole group of people. Whether you're headed on an overnight camping trip, to a tailgate party, or out to a BBQ, the comfort-grip steel handles and stainless steel lid latch make getting to your destination easy, comfortable and secure. Once you arrive, the Have-A-Seat lid will ensure you always have a place to sit and relax while you enjoy the day with a cold drink in hand. When the fun is done, the leak-resistant channel drain makes it easy to empty any excess water without tilting the cooler, so you're ready for your next fun adventure. A Coleman Steel Belted Cooler will rekindle your fondest outdoor memories  and equip you to make many new ones.

REVIEW:
The Coleman 54 quart steel-sided cooler immediately takes you back to a different time.  It is wonderfully built, with metal handles riveted to the body of the unit.  The metal latch is a major plus, as this cooler will do most of its duty for me riding in the back of a side-by-side.  While the price may initially scare some, compare this cooler with other all-plastic coolers of similar size.  The difference isn't much compared with what you get.

data.title: Coleman Hooligan Backpacking Tent
data.description: The Hooligan 2 from Coleman is a 2 person backpacking tent. It features a full coverage coated polyester fly (450mm) that envelops the tent in a cocoon of protection from the harshest weather. A small front vestibule offers some storage space for extra gear and the all mesh body is great for ventilation and stargazing on nights when the weather is clear. The fly seams are sealed at the factory and the rugged 1000D polyethylene floor is a bathtub style (wraps up the sides) for extreme weather protection and has welded seams, even in the corners, to guard against leaks. Set up is with a single pole and thus could not be more simple and quick. Great for beginner backpackers, scouts and those who want to keep it simple.Non Freestanding (1 pole) design is simple to set up and take down.All mesh body for maximum ventilation and comfort and reduces condensation.Zippered back vent in the fly helps to keep the tent comfortable - adjustable through zipper on the back tent wall.Sturdy 11mm (1ea) Shockcorded fiberglass frame for easy set up.All mesh body is perfect for stargazing on nice nights.Full coverage fly is perfect for harsh weather.1 Small storage vestibule - 6 sq. ft. - good for boots etc..1 interior storage pocket.Easy convenient continuous pole sleeve construction.Pin and ring pole to body connection is fast and simple.External guy points to keep the tent stable in storms and high winds.Instructions sewn to carry bag so they are easy to find and hard to lose.All carry sacks and stakes included.Includes convenient flashlight loop on the ceiling.Fire retardant.Import..
data.reviewText: Easy setup, spacy inside for one, but a little cramped for one adult and two kids (under 8). Condensation buildup leaves the inside of the fly wet, but on the part slanted away from you so it does not drip on your sleeping area. Overall a great tent. Was able to stow back in the case with no issues on the first time.

data.title: Coleman 9-Can Soft Cooler with Removable Liner
data.description: Keep food and drinks cold with the Coleman Soft Cooler. This compact, portable cooler bag holds up to nine cans and offers plenty of space for a family picnic or game-day snacks. Heat-welded seams in the zippered main compartment prevent leaks and maintain a cold environment, while the removable hard plastic liner fits snugly inside the soft cooler and allows you to easily dump out any ice melt. When not using the hard plastic liner, you can use ice substitutes to keep contents cold and dry. The cooler bag's easy-clean interior has an antimicrobial lining to resist formation of mold, mildew, fungus, and odor. A zippered front pocket, pocket under the lid, and two side mesh pockets make it easy to carry water bottles, snacks, silverware, and other accessories. You can also secure items to the top with the convenient bungee cords built into the lid. An adjustable shoulder strap lets you comfortably carry the cooler anywhere you go.
data.reviewText: This is a good cooler. I use it as a lunchbox for work. I like that the hard lining can be removed if needed. The only concern so far is that this cooler is so well insulated that when I leave it in the fridge, I don't believe it is letting the cold air in. My food isn't spoiling but at times, yogurt or fruit isn't as cold as if it were simply in a bag or different style box.
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman 9-Can Soft Cooler with Removable Liner

DESCRIPTION:
Keep food and drinks cold with the Coleman Soft Cooler. This compact, portable cooler bag holds up to nine cans and offers plenty of space for a family picnic or game-day snacks. Heat-welded seams in the zippered main compartment prevent leaks and maintain a cold environment, while the removable hard plastic liner fits snugly inside the soft cooler and allows you to easily dump out any ice melt. When not using the hard plastic liner, you can use ice substitutes to keep contents cold and dry. The cooler bag's easy-clean interior has an antimicrobial lining to resist formation of mold, mildew, fungus, and odor. A zippered front pocket, pocket under the lid, and two side mesh pockets make it easy to carry water bottles, snacks, silverware, and other accessories. You can also secure items to the top with the convenient bungee cords built into the lid. An adjustable shoulder strap lets you comfortably carry the cooler anywhere you go.

REVIEW:
This is a good cooler. I use it as a lunchbox for work. I like that the hard lining can be removed if needed. The only concern so far is that this cooler is so well insulated that when I leave it in the fridge, I don't believe it is letting the cold air in. My food isn't spoiling but at times, yogurt or fruit isn't as cold as if it were simply in a bag or different style box.

data.title: Coleman 8-Person Tent for Camping | Red Canyon Car Camping Tent
data.description: Measuring 17- by 10-feet, Coleman's Red Canyon tent is spacious enough to comfortably sleep eight campers. Ideal for weekend camping trips with family and friends, the Red Canyon features removable privacy walls, allowing you to create up to three separate rooms.
data.reviewText:
We upgraded to this tent as our kids got older and... well, we did too, and I wanted a cot instead of just an airbed on the ground. We are not serious campers, but wanted something with more space that we could take on our occasional trips (and set up in the backyard from time to time).

So far we have set up and broken it down 3 times, and it seems to be performing well. I just wish that more airflow was possible even with the rainfly up -- you basically have it up and no airflow, or airflow and nothing but mesh over your head -- because even if it is a beautiful day in the 70s, this tent turns into a little heat trap. Not sure if they are all like that. If nothing else, it would be nice if the rainfly was easy to pop on and off or adjust without having to do a full breakdown.

In any case, for our casual use, it has worked out just fine. No leaks, no fabric tearing, nothing crazy like that. The stakes it came with have worked, the included doormat is nice, the storage pockets are adequate, and the room "dividers" are helpful.
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman 8-Person Tent for Camping | Red Canyon Car Camping Tent

DESCRIPTION:
Measuring 17- by 10-feet, Coleman's Red Canyon tent is spacious enough to comfortably sleep eight campers. Ideal for weekend camping trips with family and friends, the Red Canyon features removable privacy walls, allowing you to create up to three separate rooms.

REVIEW:
We upgraded to this tent as our kids got older and... well, we did too, and I wanted a cot instead of just an airbed on the ground. We are not serious campers, but wanted something with more space that we could take on our occasional trips (and set up in the backyard from time to time).

So far we have set up and broken it down 3 times, and it seems to be performing well. I just wish that more airflow was possible even with the rainfly up -- you basically have it up and no airflow, or airflow and nothing but mesh over your head -- because even if it is a beautiful day in the 70s, this tent turns into a little heat trap. Not sure if they are all like that. If nothing else, it would be nice if the rainfly was easy to pop on and off or adjust without having to do a full breakdown.

In any case, for our casual use, it has worked out just fine. No leaks, no fabric tearing, nothing crazy like that. The stakes it came with have worked, the included doormat is nice, the storage pockets are adequate, and the room "dividers" are helpful.

data.title: Coleman MAX Single Burner Micro Stove Butane/Propane Model# 2000001833
data.description: Coleman Peak One Butane Stove 1-Burner, 2000001833
data.reviewText: Delivers decent performance in a small package. The fins, where the pot rests, rotate to stack on each other for storage. They have a tendency to loosen and get out of alignment, but are always easy to remedy. The valve is a bit finicky, especially at simmer. You have a fairly good range of control on this from barely on to full ahead. I also have noticed the burner itself is a bit loose on mine. I've had it for 5 years so that's really not too bad. I'd buy one again if I was in the market for a little stove.

data.title: Coleman 24-Can Party Stacker Portable Cooler
data.description: You'll be the life of the party when you take along the Coleman 24 Can Party Stacker Cooler to your next event. Not limited to canned drinks, the wide base fits 13 x 9 in. serving dishes, so you can easily pack something for everyone. No matter what you bring, the molded handles will make it easy to get it there. To add even more variety, grab a few coolers in different colors and organize your fun. Use one color to chill the adult drinks, another color for the kids, and keep food warm in yet another, so everyone will know where to find their favorite treats. When the fun is done, the specially designed base fits in the lid of other Party Stacker items, making them simple to stack and store until the next glorious get together.
data.reviewText: Great for the cost, great size, no insulation in lid
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman 24-Can Party Stacker Portable Cooler

DESCRIPTION:
You'll be the life of the party when you take along the Coleman 24 Can Party Stacker Cooler to your next event. Not limited to canned drinks, the wide base fits 13 x 9 in. serving dishes, so you can easily pack something for everyone. No matter what you bring, the molded handles will make it easy to get it there. To add even more variety, grab a few coolers in different colors and organize your fun. Use one color to chill the adult drinks, another color for the kids, and keep food warm in yet another, so everyone will know where to find their favorite treats. When the fun is done, the specially designed base fits in the lid of other Party Stacker items, making them simple to stack and store until the next glorious get together.

REVIEW:
Great for the cost, great size, no insulation in lid

data.title: Coleman Woodsman II Chair
data.description: The Coleman Woodsman II Chair is a lightweight folding chair perfect for camping, sporting events and more.Unfolded Dimensions: 15.5 in. x 17.5 in. 27.5 in. (39.4 cm x 44.5 cm x 69.9 cm).Folded Dimensions: 22 in. x 17.5 in. x 3 in. (55.9 cm x 44.5 cm x 7.6 cm).
data.reviewText: Hard to find a light portable and still comfortable camp chair that is small for packing.  Don't let little ones kneel on them. Ours put her knee though the seat the first time out with it.
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman Woodsman II Chair

DESCRIPTION:
The Coleman Woodsman II Chair is a lightweight folding chair perfect for camping, sporting events and more.Unfolded Dimensions: 15.5 in. x 17.5 in. 27.5 in. (39.4 cm x 44.5 cm x 69.9 cm).Folded Dimensions: 22 in. x 17.5 in. x 3 in. (55.9 cm x 44.5 cm x 7.6 cm).

REVIEW:
Hard to find a light portable and still comfortable camp chair that is small for packing.  Don't let little ones kneel on them. Ours put her knee though the seat the first time out with it.

data.title: Coleman Fold N Go Grill Case
data.description: Transport your Coleman Fold N Go Stove or Grill to the campsite easily with the Coleman Fold N Go Grill Case. Compatible with Coleman Fold N Go Stoves and Grills, this rugged carrying case is constructed from heavy-duty nylon and features a padded interior to keep your stove or grill protected on the go. Two drawstring side pouches allow you to easily carry extra propane cylinders (sold separately), while an interior divider keeps accessories organized and accessible. The BBQ grill bag has a durable zippered top opening and 2 strong web handles for comfortable carrying.
data.reviewText: The fit is just right - big enough for easy in and out of the grill but small enough to hold everything in place, including the auxiliary griddle we bought separately. Solid construction, convenient carry straps, and pockets for two gas bottles. Keeps everything together and tidy in our RV.
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman Fold N Go Grill Case

DESCRIPTION:
Transport your Coleman Fold N Go Stove or Grill to the campsite easily with the Coleman Fold N Go Grill Case. Compatible with Coleman Fold N Go Stoves and Grills, this rugged carrying case is constructed from heavy-duty nylon and features a padded interior to keep your stove or grill protected on the go. Two drawstring side pouches allow you to easily carry extra propane cylinders (sold separately), while an interior divider keeps accessories organized and accessible. The BBQ grill bag has a durable zippered top opening and 2 strong web handles for comfortable carrying.

REVIEW:
The fit is just right - big enough for easy in and out of the grill but small enough to hold everything in place, including the auxiliary griddle we bought separately. Solid construction, convenient carry straps, and pockets for two gas bottles. Keeps everything together and tidy in our RV.

data.title: Coleman Pack-Away Deluxe Portable Kitchen
data.description:
Bring a cooking station with full features to your next outdoor gathering with the Coleman Pack-Away Deluxe Portable Kitchen. Equipped with a 39.4 x 19.7-inch food prep area, this portable camp kitchen offers ample space for slicing meats and chopping vegetables. A removable sink easily slots into the prep area for easy cleaning. The side stand accommodates most Coleman stoves and coolers, while hanging hooks, a paper-towel holder, and wire shelves offer additional storage space for plates, utensils, and ingredients. To facilitate late-night cooking, a lantern hook provides a convenient place to hang a light. Setting up this portable kitchen is easy: Simply expand the aluminum frame, snap on the surfaces, and make sure the kitchen's footing is steady. When it's time to pack up, the kitchen folds up and easily fits into a single, self-contained case for easy carrying and storage. With its lightweight aluminum frame and sturdy construction, this folding camp kitchen is built to withstand years of rugged use. Outdoor cooking is a ton of fun, but it's not always easy to prep your meal on coolers, picnic tables, and car tailgates. Enter the Coleman deluxe Camp Kitchen, a complete outdoor food preparation center that fits inside a compact carrying case.


The Camp Kitchen includes a removable dish tub for post-meal cleanup.
The Camp Kitchen is outfitted with a rust-resistant aluminum frame made using heavy-duty channel-extruded aluminum for added strength. More important than the construction, however, is the functionality. The kitchen's main body includes a moisture-resistant MDF tabletop food prep area with plenty of space for chopping onions and shaping hamburger patties. To the side is a wire-grid extender shelf that easily holds most Coleman and other brands of camping stoves at countertop level. No more cooking on the ground or an unstable stump. It's the extras that really make the Camp Kitchen go. The unit offers a handy bottom shelf for storing items like pots, dishware, and bread, along with chrome-plated wire hooks and a spice rack/shelves along the back for mugs, spatulas, and condiments. The Camp Kitchen even includes a removable tub for washing your dishes and a produce bin. And when you're ready to head home, the Camp Kitchen folds up into an easy-to-carry suitcase-style frame that fits easily in most car trunks. Other details include stable leg braces for added support, plastic guards to protect the corners, a lantern hook, a paper-towel holder, and a hand-towel holder. The Camp Kitchen measures 74.5 by 64 by 20 inches (W x H x D) when open and 39.5 by 7 by 20 inches when closed and weighs 24.2 pounds. About Coleman
More than 100 years ago, a young man with an entrepreneurial spirit and a better idea began manufacturing lanterns in Wichita, Kansas. His name was W.C. Coleman, and the company he founded would change life in America. A man plagued with such poor vision he sometimes had to ask classmates to read aloud to him, Coleman saw a brilliant light in 1900 in a drugstore window that stopped him in his tracks. He inquired about the light inside and discovered he was able to read even the small print on a medicine bottle by the illumination. The lamps had mantles, not wicks, and were fueled by gasoline under pressure instead of coal oil. Soon afterward, Coleman started a lighting service that offered a "no light, no pay" clause--a big step forward for merchants who were burned by inferior products that rarely worked--and drew substantial interest from businesses that wanted to keep their lights on after dark. In the ensuing years, Coleman expanded its product line well beyond lanterns. The company's current catalog is thick with products that make spending time outdoors a pleasure. There are coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days on end, comfortable airbeds that won't deflate during the night, a complete line of LED lights that last for years, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and much, much more. Coleman has truly fashioned much of our outdoor camping experience, and expects to do so for generations to come.
data.reviewText: Open soon and check for defects.  Mine had cracks I didn't see until I went camping months later.  Check it out.

data.title: Coleman Aluminum Deck Chair with Swivel Table
data.description: Experience comfortable outdoor dining in the Coleman Aluminum Deck Chair with Swivel Table. Its built-in side table lets you enjoy a meal or snack from the comfort of your seat, with a versatile cup holder that can hold a bottle, can, wine glass, or coffee cup. When you're finished eating, the table swivels 360 degrees to stay out of your way. This camping chairs strong aluminum frame, ergonomically angled seat, cushioned back, and padded armrests encourage hours of comfortable relaxation on the deck, at the campsite, or in the backyard. This outdoor folding chair has sewn-in carry straps for easy transportation and storage. It supports up to 225 pounds and is constructed from weather-resistant polyester for durability.
data.reviewText: ripped the ugly white tags off and pulled out all the stitching on one side, terrible design.

data.title: Coleman Steel-Belted Portable Cooler, 54 Quart
data.description: Go back in time with cold food and drinks today when you take a Coleman 54 Quart Steel Belted Cooler along with you. It looks just like the one you remember with its classic design first developed in 1954. Large enough to hold 85 cans and tall enough to hold 2-liter bottles upright, you'll have plenty of space for refreshments for a whole group of people. Whether you're headed on an overnight camping trip, to a tailgate party, or out to a BBQ, the comfort-grip steel handles and stainless steel lid latch make getting to your destination easy, comfortable and secure. Once you arrive, the Have-A-Seat lid will ensure you always have a place to sit and relax while you enjoy the day with a cold drink in hand. When the fun is done, the leak-resistant channel drain makes it easy to empty any excess water without tilting the cooler, so you're ready for your next fun adventure. A Coleman Steel Belted Cooler will rekindle your fondest outdoor memories  and equip you to make many new ones.
data.reviewText: I didn't buy mine on Amazon but have had one for around 4 years.  Mine is all stainless steel which gets hot but somehow keeps ice for days.  I have a Coleman 120 quart that advertises keeps ice for 6 days. Not even close.  After 3 days all the ice was melted in the 120 quart and steel one still had ice.  The big disappoint for me is my latch is steel but the hook is plastic.  After a few uses I could open the lid without turning the latch open.  Important for float trips if you turn the canoe over.  All my stuff in the cooler fell out into the river.  I hate plastic parts on pieces that get used repeatedly.
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman Steel-Belted Portable Cooler, 54 Quart

DESCRIPTION:
Go back in time with cold food and drinks today when you take a Coleman 54 Quart Steel Belted Cooler along with you. It looks just like the one you remember with its classic design first developed in 1954. Large enough to hold 85 cans and tall enough to hold 2-liter bottles upright, you'll have plenty of space for refreshments for a whole group of people. Whether you're headed on an overnight camping trip, to a tailgate party, or out to a BBQ, the comfort-grip steel handles and stainless steel lid latch make getting to your destination easy, comfortable and secure. Once you arrive, the Have-A-Seat lid will ensure you always have a place to sit and relax while you enjoy the day with a cold drink in hand. REVIEW:
I didn't buy mine on Amazon but have had one for around 4 years.  Mine is all stainless steel which gets hot but somehow keeps ice for days.  I have a Coleman 120 quart that advertises keeps ice for 6 days. Not even close.  After 3 days all the ice was melted in the 120 quart and steel one still had ice.  The big disappoint for me is my latch is steel but the hook is plastic.  After a few uses I could open the lid without turning the latch open.  Important for float trips if you turn the canoe over.  All my stuff in the cooler fell out into the river.  I hate plastic parts on pieces that get used repeatedly.

data.title: Coleman 100-Quart Xtreme 5-Day Heavy-Duty Cooler with Wheels, Blue
data.description:
Food, drinksand the funlast longer, when you roll in with the Coleman 100-Quart Xtreme 5-Day Heavy-Duty Cooler with Wheels. To be exact, this cooler keeps ice up to 5 days at temps as high as 90F thanks to an insulated lid and extra insulation in the walls. Large enough to hold 160 cans, this spacious cooler transports and chills enough food and drinks so the entire party remains fueled and refreshed for the duration of your adventures. Wherever youre headedcampgrounds, tailgate parking lot, or across a ball fieldthe heavy-duty wheels and large tow handle let you roll over almost any terrain with little effort. The additional 2-way side handles make it easier to lift your cooler in and out of the car or onto a picnic bench. Once you arrive, the Have-A-Seat lid ensures you have a place to sit and relax, and the cup holders molded into the lid keep your drink close and prevent spills. When the fun is done, the leak-resistant channel drain lets you empty any excess water without tilting the cooler, so youre ready for your next fun adventure in no time. Carry up to 160 cans with ease--and store ice for up to five days even when it's 90 degrees outside--in the Xtreme 5 100-Quart Wheeled Cooler by Coleman. With two large wheels, two-way handles and a comfortable tow handle for one-hand operation, this cooler is easy to roll even on rough terrain. After the party, it's easy to drain thanks to a rustproof, leak-resistant channel drain for no-tilt draining. Features:
  • Keeps ice up to 5 days at 90 degrees Fahrenheit
  • Holds 160 cans
  • Hinged lid with beverage holders
  • Two-way handles for flexible easy carrying
  • Comfortable tow handle for one-hand operation
  • Rustproof, leak-resistant channel drain for no-tilt easy draining
  • Two large wheels for easy pulling, even on rough terrain
  • Made with Thermozone insulation that contains no toxic CFCs, HFCs, or HCFCs
Specifications:
  • Exterior Dimensions: 36.5 by 16.75 by 18.25 inches
  • Interior Dimensions: 23.75 by 11.75 by 13 inches
  • Weight Empty: 18 pounds
  • Made in the USA
About Coleman
More than 100 years ago, a young man with an entrepreneurial spirit and a better idea began manufacturing lanterns in Wichita, Kansas. His name was W.C. Coleman, and the company he founded would change life in America. A man plagued with such poor vision he sometimes had to ask classmates to read aloud to him, Coleman saw a brilliant light in 1900 in a drugstore window that stopped him in his tracks. He inquired about the light inside and discovered he was able to read even the small print on a medicine bottle by the illumination. The lamps had mantles, not wicks, and were fueled by gasoline under pressure instead of coal oil. Soon afterward, Coleman started a lighting service that offered a "no light, no pay" clause--a big step forward for merchants who were burned by inferior products that rarely worked--and drew substantial interest from businesses that wanted to keep their lights on after dark. In the ensuing years, Coleman expanded its product line well beyond lanterns. The company's current catalog is thick with products that make spending time outdoors a pleasure. There are coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days on end, comfortable airbeds that won't deflate during the night, a complete line of LED lights that last for years, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and much, much more. Coleman has truly fashioned much of our outdoor camping experience, and expects to do so for generations to come.
data.reviewText: Dont buy this product if you plan on using it more than once. Actually, just don't waste your money on this at all. The hinges on ours broke off the first time we used it and the only way we could fix it was by putting one back on sideways making it impossible to open all the way and replacement hinges won't stay in. The handle broke off when we were moving it. We did tons of research to figure out the best cooler for us and landed on this one. BIG MISTAKE. Now we have a broken piece of junk thats barely holding together and were out over $100 dollars! We were really hoping that spending more money and buying a Coleman product would be worth it. This product is just disappointing and cheaply made.

data.title: Coleman Oak Point Cool-Weather Big and Tall Sleeping Bag
data.description: Stay comfortable during chilly nights outdoors with the Coleman Oak Point Cool-Weather Big and Tall Sleeping Bag. This lightweight sleeping bag has heat-locking construction with a polyester cover, Coletherm insulation, and a soft cotton flannel lining to keep you warm in temperatures as low as 30F. Fiberlock construction keeps the insulation from shifting, reducing cold spots and increasing durability. For additional warmth, a Thermolock draft tube helps prevent heat from escaping through the zipper. The 2-way patented ZipPlow system plows fabric away from the zipper to prevent snags and frustration. And thanks to its Roll Control design and Quick Cord patented no-tie closure, this backpacking sleeping bag is easy for one person to pack without assistance. This adult sleeping bag can accommodate most people up to 6 feet 4 inches tall and is machine washable for easy cleaning.
data.reviewText: Needs more filling for posted temps and needs a collar or drawer string at the neck to cut drafts
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman Oak Point Cool-Weather Big and Tall Sleeping Bag

DESCRIPTION:
Stay comfortable during chilly nights outdoors with the Coleman Oak Point Cool-Weather Big and Tall Sleeping Bag. This lightweight sleeping bag has heat-locking construction with a polyester cover, Coletherm insulation, and a soft cotton flannel lining to keep you warm in temperatures as low as 30F. Fiberlock construction keeps the insulation from shifting, reducing cold spots and increasing durability. For additional warmth, a Thermolock draft tube helps prevent heat from escaping through the zipper. The 2-way patented ZipPlow system plows fabric away from the zipper to prevent snags and frustration. And thanks to its Roll Control design and Quick Cord patented no-tie closure, this backpacking sleeping bag is easy for one person to pack without assistance. This adult sleeping bag can accommodate most people up to 6 feet 4 inches tall and is machine washable for easy cleaning.

REVIEW:
Needs more filling for posted temps and needs a collar or drawer string at the neck to cut drafts

data.title: Coleman Dunnock Cold Weather Adult Sleeping Bag
data.description: Ideal for camping at higher altitudes or in cooler months, the Coleman Dunnock Cold-Weather Sleeping Bag helps you sleep comfortably in temperatures as low as 20F. This sleeping bag features Fiberlock construction that keeps the insulation from shifting, reducing cold spots and increasing durability. A cotton cover and ultra-soft cotton flannel liner also keep you warm, as well as a Thermolock draft tube that prevents heat loss through the zipper. The bottom of the bag unzips to provide extra ventilation on warmer nights. The 2-way patented ZipPlow system plows fabric away from the zipper to prevent snags and frustration. Plus, the Roll Control design and Wrap 'N' Roll integrated packing system make this lightweight backpacking sleeping bag easy to roll and pack. This sleeping bag has a spacious design that can accommodate most people up to 5 feet 11 inches tall and is machine washable for easy cleaning.
data.reviewText: I wish they would be a bit more descriptive in their descriptions of these.  This has the cotton feel exterior and interior.  i was hoping for the silky, smooth, polyester.

data.title: Coleman Beach Shade
data.description: " Take your own shady, private space to the beach or park with the Coleman Beach Shade. This pop-up beach tent is made with UVGuard material that offers 50+ UPF protection from the suns harmful rays and durable Polyguard material that is designed to last season after season. The extended floor can be zipped shut for privacy while changing clothes, and the dry line offers a convenient place to hang wet bathing suits and towels. Integrated storage pockets give you a secure place to put your items while you swim or sunbathe. And when it gets hot, the back window can be unzipped to let the breeze in, while the sand bag compartments can be filled as needed to anchor the beach canopy on windy days. The Coleman Beach Shade comes with ground stakes and pre-attached guy lines and can be set up in about five minutes.
data.reviewText:
It is OK once it is put together but it is one pain in the neck to put up.
The sand bag idea was not thought out. It needs to be secure to the ground before you can even start thinking about erecting it. We had a hard time with it using the stakes into dirt so I know it would not be fun putting up on the beach. We wound up ordering another tent. I wish I could return it.
UPDATE 2013 !!!!! We had this tent for a couple a years now and never used it again so we gave it to my Daughter to try and use it on the beach for her two small girls. She could not put it together and gave it back to us.
We would not recommend it and wish we never bought this wast of money tent. ( We hope Coleman reads this and discontinues it )
Brian Cianfrani
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman Beach Shade

DESCRIPTION:
" Take your own shady, private space to the beach or park with the Coleman Beach Shade. This pop-up beach tent is made with UVGuard material that offers 50+ UPF protection from the suns harmful rays and durable Polyguard material that is designed to last season after season. The extended floor can be zipped shut for privacy while changing clothes, and the dry line offers a convenient place to hang wet bathing suits and towels. Integrated storage pockets give you a secure place to put your items while you swim or sunbathe. And when it gets hot, the back window can be unzipped to let the breeze in, while the sand bag compartments can be filled as needed to anchor the beach canopy on windy days. The Coleman Beach Shade comes with ground stakes and pre-attached guy lines and can be set up in about five minutes.

REVIEW:
It is OK once it is put together but it is one pain in the neck to put up.
The sand bag idea was not thought out. It needs to be secure to the ground before you can even start thinking about erecting it. We had a hard time with it using the stakes into dirt so I know it would not be fun putting up on the beach. We wound up ordering another tent. I wish I could return it.
UPDATE 2013 !!!!! We had this tent for a couple a years now and never used it again so we gave it to my Daughter to try and use it on the beach for her two small girls. She could not put it together and gave it back to us.
We would not recommend it and wish we never bought this wast of money tent. ( We hope Coleman reads this and discontinues it )
Brian Cianfrani

data.title: Coleman RoadTrip LXX Portable Propane Grill, Black
data.description: Show off your grilling skills on camping trips or at tailgate parties with the Coleman RoadTrip LXE Portable Propane Grill. This outdoor grill combines a collapsible, portable design with convenient features that facilitate grilling in tough conditions. The grill's individually adjustable dual burners each blast out 10,000 BTUs of heat, giving you enough cooking power to prepare a wide range of foods, from grilled veggies to thick slabs of steak. The Swaptop interchangeable design allows you to switch out the grill grates for a griddle top or stove grate (both sold separately), while the Instastart ignition lets you light the burners by simply pushing a button. Ideal for summer cookouts, this portable gas grill also excels in chilly conditions thanks to PerfectFlow technology, which helps ensure steady heat output. Sliding tables on both sides of the grill provide easy access to ingredients and utensils and can be tucked away for easy transport and storage. With its 285-square-inch cooktop, this grill offers a large cooking surface area without sacrificing portability. Its collapsible stand sets up in seconds and folds up for compact storage, while its large handle and wheels make the grill easy to transport. And thanks to its porcelain-coated cast iron grates and removable grease tray, this camp grill is easy to clean.
data.reviewText: The first time using the grill it worked great. The second time I tried to use it the burner I cooked over the FIRST time would no longer ignite on its own! Due to Chicago winters I wasn't able to figure all this out before the return window ended

data.title: Coleman Camping Instant Signal Mountain Tent
data.reviewText:
This is a huge tent that after you get the hang of it, goes up pretty easily. It's just that first time you do it the instructions aren't very good or is the process very intuitive.  That said here are the pros and cons of this tent as I see them.

Pros:
* Goes up quickly. After getting the hang of it that is.
* No poles to have to fold/unfold and keep track up.
* Guy lines are pre-attached.
* Tent floor is heavier duty tarp material obviating the need for a separate tarp.
* Big windows
* Two tent pockets inside tent
* Nice color scheme, though obviously a personal opinion.
* No rainfly required. (more on this below)
* Carrying/packing bag includes handles and tension buckles.

Cons:
* Seems to be some quality control issues.  My tent came with one of the corners misassembled at one of the bottom corners.  It was an easy fix to untangle the tent once I figured out what was going on.  Other posters have posted similar fixable but missed QC issues.
* More QC issues.  There seems to be some alignment issues with some of the window panels as they zipper really pulls against the stitching in a few spots.  I'm pretty sure this won't last long.
* No rainfly included.  Yes, you don't have to mess with installing a separate rainfly but you also can't open the windows if it's raining or you are planning on it raining.  There is a ventilation flap at one end of the tent and you could open the door at the top to mesh to get some cross-draft going but ventilation is seriously limited in this regard.
* Straps go through the tent at the corners.  Strangely the tent strap go through a flap at each corner into the tent leaving small bug entry paths into the tent.  My kids point them out when they went back into the tent and there were a few fruit flys on the inside.

To be determined:
* Like some other users, the tent poles collapsed a couple times on my kids when they were playing in the tent. When I went to inspect the poles they seemed quite sturdy.  Not sure if it was an issue of not seating them properly the first time or not.

Summary:
This is a big, airy (when it's not raining) tent that's easy to set up and take down but seems to suffer from a couple odd design choices and some random quality control.

Advice on usage:
Don't close up the windows when packing the tent away as it keeps the air from escaping and makes the tent much more difficult to roll up.  Otherwise it rolls up fairly well and easily fits back into the carrying bag.

data.title: Coleman Camping Instant Signal Mountain Tent
data.reviewText:
Not so sure about this one. It was relatively easy to set up, thanks to the built in poles. My middle schooler did not have enough strength to clip in the clips though, despite being plenty strong. I like the way the instructions are sewn into the carry bag and printed on a waterproof material. The instructions were a bit off though, they told me to connect poles for the "rain tarp" that were already connected at the factory.

Speaking of which, the "rain tarp" is just a triangle that covers over the single door. If it was raining you would need to close all the other windows or you would get drenched. There is a little vent that is below the back window that has a rain cover (if you stake it out) but still  I can't imagine this being comfortable in a humid rainy situation.

I like the center ceiling mounted o-ring that will allow for a lantern to be hung. It has 2 sewn in patches for your wallet and phone and such which is nice.

Of course you know that take the # of people a tent says it can fit and divide by half to see what it will actually comfortably accommodate.

It has a "power port" a little zipper in the corner that opens and allows a cord to come in. Not sure I'd want A/C power coming into my tent in the rain - but this may also be useful to pass small items back and forth from the outside without having to open the whole door.

Folding up is kind of another issue. The instructions just say "reverse the setup procedure" but after all of the origami like movements you have to do, it's hard to remember what it looked like at the start and to get it back to a place where it fits in the bag. I'd easily say that the time I saved not having to take out the poles and fold them was eaten up by trying to figure out how to fold the thing and stuff it back in the carry bag. I suspect this would improve over time and with experience.

Also, the "1 minute" to set up is a bit disingenuous. Maybe if you really have your act together, but no way does this time include setting up all the necessary stakes for the tent corners and guy wires.

Overall, a great experience setting up, a little less so breaking down. Could prevent many family fights at the campsite ;-)

data.title: Coleman Jug With Water Carrier, 5 Gallons, Blue
data.description: Coleman, 5 gallon, blue, poly Lite water carrier, molded in handle & hand grip, fast flow spigot stores on the handle when not in use, non-insulated, polyethylene case resists scratches & dents. Comfortably port water to your campsite, picnic, boat, and beyond with the Coleman 5-gallon water carrier. The carrier features a tough polyethylene construction that's scratch- and impact-resistant, helping it hold up to rough handling. More importantly, the carrier's fast-flow spigot delivers instant water access; no more struggling to tip the carrier each time you fill up your water bottle. The carrier also includes a built-in molded handle and handgrip for comfortable, easy carrying.

data.title: Coleman Camp Stove Carry Case, Medium
data.description: Take your Coleman stove wherever outdoor adventures lead you with the Coleman Camp Stove Carry Case, Medium. Designed to fit Coleman 2-burner stoves measuring up to 21.5 x 14 x 4.8 inches, this rugged carry case made from PVC-coated polyester protects your stove during transportation and storage. Strong web handles provide comfort when toting stoves between campsites, while the durable zipper is designed to keep stoves safe and dry. Two propane pouches with drawstrings securely carry extra fuel canisters (sold separately).

data.title: Coleman Camp Axe
data.description: Get the right tool for multiple jobs when you're at the campsite with the Coleman Camp Axe. The sharp edge of the durable drop-forged steel head easily splits wood for a campfire or stove, while the flat edge works wonders for driving tent stakes into hard ground. When it's time to pack up, pulling your tent stakes is easy with the notch cut into the head. Durable and well built, the Coleman Camp Axe is perfect for the campsite, wooded terrain, or the backyard. The axe is equipped with a drop-forged carbon-steel axe head that easily splits wood into kindling, along with a forged steel handle and an ergonomic nonslip grip. With the Coleman Camp Axe, you'll be ready for any camping contingency.
data.reviewText: It's cheap, but it really isn't worth it. The balance is poor, and the blade comes dull as a hammer. Spend the extra $10-15 and get a better hatchet.
data.text:

data.title: Coleman CPX 6 Rechargeable Power Cartridge
data.description: Power all your Coleman CPX 6 gear with the Coleman CPX 6 Rechargeable Power Cartridge. This rechargeable battery kit allows you to make any Coleman CPX 6 product rechargeable, helping you save money on disposable batteries and minimize waste in landfills. Each kit includes a rechargeable battery, a 12-volt adapter for convenient vehicle recharging, and a 120-volt adapter for use with conventional AC outlets. Built to resist impacts and water, the battery features a rugged design and comes fitted with a rubber plug to protect against dirt and debris. It recharges quickly via either a vehicle or home outlet. And thanks to the power cartridge's charge indicator, you'll know exactly when the battery is fully charged.
data.reviewText: Battery pack gives good power for about 2-3 hours and then slowly starts to die out.  I also bought EBL rechargeable D batteries and they ran strong for 10 plus hours, I would invest your money in rechargeable D batteries.

data.title: Coleman Cooler Stainless Steel Hinges and Screws
data.description: For replacement on most Coleman Coolers with plastic hinges as well as Coleman steel Coolers made after 2000.
data.reviewText: The hinges are an excellent fit, and seem to be reasonably good quality.  The factory plastic hinges that Coleman uses are absolutely worthless, and I've had them snap apart on multiple coolers for multiple reasons.  These seem like they will last a while - will be especially interested in how they hold up in a salt air environment.  My only complaint is the price.  I got my cooler on sale (from Amazon); these hinges (from a third-party Amazon affiliate, I think) cost just under half the price of the cooler itself.
